title: Add 'just ci' — full local mirror of the GitHub Actions pipeline (Actions unavailable on this account)
description: GitHub Actions jobs fail at start: 'recent account payments have failed or your spending limit needs to be increased', and billing won't be changed. Mitigate by mirroring .github/workflows/ci.yml locally as 'just ci' (modeled on chorez's justfile): ci-backend (cargo build+test --locked), ci-frontend (frozen install + svelte-check + build), e2e (release binary on :3002 with runtime-minted hash, Playwright smoke), with chorez-style port-scoped preflight, bounded server wait, and PID cleanup. Document as the PR gate while Actions is down.

title: Fix duplicate open_app_with_db in tests/common (PR #35 + #48 textual merge broke main CI)
description: PR #35 (kammerz-8rh) added open_app_with_db() -> (Router, DatabaseConnection) and PR #48 (kammerz-u29) added open_app_with_db(db_url) -> Router; both merged into tests/common/mod.rs without textual conflict, so cargo test fails to compile on main (E0428) and all CI on main + open PRs is red. Fix: rename the URL-taking variant to open_app_with_url and update its callers (tests/backup.rs).

title: Enforce lab/self dev mutual exclusion in the backend (both-dev rolls strand status on the wrong flow)
description:
The 'lab and self dev are mutually exclusive' invariant (CLAUDE.md) is enforced only by the UI hiding '+ Lab'/'+ Self' buttons. The backend create handlers never check the sibling table: src/routes/development.rs:147-187 (create_lab_dev) and :304-358 (create_self_dev) insert unconditionally. Migration 19 (migration/src/m20260222_000019_schema_hardening.rs:25-26) makes each dev type unique per roll, but nothing prevents one lab dev AND one self dev on the same roll.

Reachable in practice: this app is used from a phone in the field and a desktop — a stale tab on one device still shows the '+ Self' button after the other device created a lab dev. Also reachable via raw API.

Verified consequences once both records exist:
1. Status stays on the first path: sync on create no-ops cross-flow (advance_status_along, src/services/roll_service.rs:251-276, returns Ok(false) when current status isn't in the target flow).
2. Deleting either record strands the roll: delete_lab_dev (development.rs:269-283) and delete_self_dev (:458-472) skip the revert-to-shot when the sibling exists, so e.g. a roll at 'at-lab' whose lab dev is deleted keeps status 'at-lab' with only a self dev. No auto-sync can ever fix it: resync_self_dev_status advances only along SELF_FLOW (at-lab is off-flow) and reverts only from Developed (roll_service.rs:336-353). Only a manual chevron click recovers it — and the chevron bar renders it confusingly (getDevPath prioritizes lab when both exist; statusFlow.indexOf(status) === -1 makes every chevron look 'future' and every click an unconfirmed forward write, frontend/src/routes/(app)/rolls/[id]/+page.svelte:112,487-488,546).

Why it matters for first release: this is the core lifecycle invariant of the user's #1-concern state machine, and the strandable states are silent (no error, just a status that contradicts the data and stops auto-syncing).

Suggested fix: inside the existing create transactions, count sibling rows (development_self for create_lab_dev, development_lab for create_self_dev) and return AppError::UnprocessableEntity ('This roll already has a self/lab development record — delete it first') when one exists. That makes the sibling-skip branches in the delete handlers dead code for new data; keep them as defense-in-depth or replace with a reconcile to the surviving sibling's data-driven target. Add backend tests: create-sibling rejected; (if keeping reconcile) delete-with-sibling lands on the survivor's flow.
notes: verifier: confirmed — create_lab_dev/create_self_dev (src/routes/development.rs:147,304) insert with no sibling-table check; delete handlers (:269-283,:458-472) skip the →shot revert when a sibling exists, leaving e.g. 'at-lab' with only a self dev; advance_status_along (roll_service.rs:262-275) no-ops off-flow so no resync ever repairs it; and rolls/[id]/+page.svelte handleStatusClick treats currentStatusIdx===-1 as forward (no ConfirmDialog). Stale-tab on the documented phone+desktop usage makes it reachable. P1 stands — core invariant, silent corruption, cheap pre-release fix.

title: Search results link to nonexistent lens/film-stock/lab detail routes (404)
description:
Search result cards link to detail pages that do not exist, so clicking them dead-ends on the SvelteKit 404 error page.

Evidence:
- frontend/src/routes/(app)/search/+page.svelte:131 — lenses link to href="/lenses/{lens.id}"
- frontend/src/routes/(app)/search/+page.svelte:164 — film stocks link to href="/film-stocks/{fs.id}"
- frontend/src/routes/(app)/search/+page.svelte:266 — labs link to href="/labs/{lab.id}"
- The route tree has only frontend/src/routes/(app)/lenses/+page.svelte, film-stocks/+page.svelte, labs/+page.svelte — no [id] subroutes for any of them (cameras and rolls do have [id] pages and their search links work).

Why it matters for first release: search is a primary navigation surface; half its result sections are broken links. On a phone over VPN this looks like the app crashed.

Suggested fix: link these three sections to their list pages (e.g. /lenses, /film-stocks, /labs — optionally with ?q= to pre-filter via the ListToolbar search), or add the missing [id] detail routes. The lens row also re-implements lensDisplayName()'s brand-doubling guard inline (line 135); if LensSearchResult can't reuse the util, extract a small shared helper while in there.
notes: verifier: confirmed — search/+page.svelte lines 131/164/266 link to /lenses/{id}, /film-stocks/{id}, /labs/{id}; route tree has no [id] subroutes under lenses/, film-stocks/, labs/ (only +page.svelte each). Cameras/rolls links (lines 100/195/232) target real [id] routes. P1 appropriate: broken navigation on a primary surface.

title: Add a responsive/mobile layout — fixed 224px sidebar and fixed-column grids make the app unusable on phones
description:
The app is explicitly used IN THE FIELD ON A PHONE over VPN, but the frontend has essentially zero responsive design. A grep for Tailwind breakpoint prefixes (sm:/md:/lg:/xl:) across frontend/src finds only two real uses, both in stats/+page.svelte (lines 146, 220); the Button.svelte matches are object keys, not breakpoints.

Concrete problems:
- frontend/src/lib/components/layout/Sidebar.svelte:28 — nav is a fixed 'w-56' (224px) column with no collapse/drawer behavior; frontend/src/routes/(app)/+layout.svelte:7-11 lays it out as 'flex h-screen' beside main. On a 390px phone viewport the content area is left ~166px wide on every page.
- frontend/src/routes/(app)/quick-entry/+page.svelte:297 — the core field-logging form is 'grid grid-cols-4' (Frame / f/ / Speed / Lens). Four columns in ~166px is unusable; this is THE page designed for phone use.
- frontend/src/routes/(app)/+page.svelte:102,201 and stats/+page.svelte:54 — 'grid grid-cols-4' stat cards with no breakpoint fallback.
- frontend/src/routes/(app)/rolls/[id]/+page.svelte:687,703,980,985 and rolls/new/+page.svelte:251, DevelopmentSection.svelte:355,377,384 — 2/3-column form grids inside dialogs and edit panes, no collapse.
- frontend/src/routes/(app)/rolls/[id]/+page.svelte:779-842 — the status chevron bar renders up to 7 fixed-padding segments in a nowrap flex row (~500px min); on a phone the buttons get crushed/clipped with no overflow-x-auto or wrap.
- frontend/src/lib/components/ui/ListToolbar.svelte:38-86 — search input + two min-w-[120px] selects + count in one nowrap row.
- frontend/src/lib/components/ui/Dialog.svelte:29,40 — backdrop has no padding, so 'w-full max-w-lg' dialogs are edge-to-edge below 512px.

Why it matters: the stated deployment story (NAS + VPN + phone in the field) is broken at first release without this; viewport meta is correct (app.html) so the layout genuinely renders at device width.

Suggested fix: below md, replace the persistent sidebar with a hamburger-drawer or bottom tab bar; change fixed grids to 'grid-cols-2 md:grid-cols-4' (and 1→2/3 for form grids); give the chevron bar overflow-x-auto or wrap; add p-4 to dialog backdrops; let ListToolbar wrap. No backend changes needed.
notes:
verifier: confirmed — grep shows only stats/+page.svelte uses breakpoints; Sidebar.svelte:28 w-56 fixed in (app)/+layout.svelte flex h-screen; quick-entry:297 grid-cols-4; rolls/[id]:779-842 nowrap chevron bar; Dialog.svelte:29 backdrop unpadded; ListToolbar nowrap row. Viewport meta correct, so layout genuinely breaks at 390px. P1 stands given phone-in-field is the stated deployment.
merged kammerz-xys (workflow-ux finder, independently found): roll-lifecycle specifics — non-wrapping 8-segment chevron bar, fixed w-56 sidebar, hover-only controls; lifecycle pages are the worst-hit field workflows

title: Roll lifecycle UI is unusable at phone widths (fixed sidebar + non-wrapping chevron bar)
description: The deployment story is field use over VPN, often on a phone, but the roll lifecycle journey has no responsive handling at all. Evidence: frontend/src/routes/(app)/+layout.svelte:7-10 wraps everything in 'flex h-screen overflow-hidden' with a fixed w-56 (224px) Sidebar (frontend/src/lib/components/layout/Sidebar.svelte:29), leaving ~166px of content on a 390px phone. The status chevron bar — the primary lifecycle control — renders up to 8 segments in 'flex items-center gap-[2px]' with no flex-wrap and no overflow-x-auto (frontend/src/routes/(app)/rolls/[id]/+page.svelte:779-842), so labels like Post-processed get crushed/clipped and later chevrons (Scanned/Archived) are effectively unreachable. grep shows only stats/+page.svelte and Button.svelte use any sm:/md:/lg: classes anywhere in the app. Why it matters: the user's #1 workflow (advance a roll from the field) physically does not fit on the device it is meant to be used from; this is a first-release blocker-adjacent gap. Suggested fix: (1) collapsible sidebar (hamburger) below md:, (2) make the chevron bar horizontally scrollable (overflow-x-auto + whitespace-nowrap, scroll current status into view) or wrap to two rows at narrow widths, (3) audit roll detail dialogs/grids (grid-cols-3 shot form) for narrow viewports.
notes: verifier: confirmed — (app)/+layout.svelte:7-9 fixed flex h-screen overflow-hidden with w-56 Sidebar (Sidebar.svelte:28, no breakpoints), chevron bar at rolls/[id]/+page.svelte:779 is 'flex items-center gap-[2px]' with no flex-wrap/overflow-x-auto; repo-wide grep shows only Button.svelte and stats/+page.svelte use sm:/md:/lg:. ~166px content width on a 390px phone makes the 7-8 segment bar unusable; P1 is right given the stated phone-in-the-field deployment.
close_reason: duplicate of kammerz-c6o (app-wide responsive/mobile layout); chevron-bar specifics merged there

title: Stats endpoint 500s on partial date_loaded (YYYY-MM) and emits garbage months for year-only dates
description:
GET /api/stats breaks when any roll has a partial date_loaded, which validate.rs (src/validate.rs:8-10) explicitly accepts as valid input (YYYY and YYYY-MM).

Evidence (verified against SQLite):
- src/services/stats_service.rs:109-120 (rolls_per_month query) uses STRFTIME('%Y-%m', date_loaded). For 'YYYY-MM' values SQLite returns NULL; the result row has month=NULL, and MonthCount.month is a non-Option String (src/services/stats_service.rs:18), so FromQueryResult fails with a DbErr -> AppError::Internal -> the whole /api/stats response is a 500. One roll loaded as '2026-06' bricks the stats page.
- For year-only 'YYYY' values, SQLite interprets the bare digits as a Julian day number: STRFTIME('%Y-%m','2026') = '-4707-06', producing a garbage month bucket in the chart.
- The WHERE filter date_loaded >= DATE('now','-12 months') is a lexicographic TEXT comparison, so partial dates are also included/excluded incorrectly (e.g. '2025' < '2025-06-11' is excluded even if conceptually in range).

Why it matters: partial dates are a first-class supported feature (kammerz-igc validation work), and a single such roll makes the entire stats endpoint return 500 — the dashboard stats are unusable until the date is edited.

Suggested fix: restrict the month bucketing to full dates (WHERE date_loaded LIKE '____-__-__') or bucket via SUBSTR(date_loaded,1,7) only when LENGTH(date_loaded) >= 7, and/or make MonthCount.month an Option<String> and drop NULL rows. Add a regression test with a 'YYYY' and a 'YYYY-MM' date_loaded roll.
notes: verifier: confirmed — reproduced in sqlite3: STRFTIME('%Y-%m','2026-06')=NULL (fails non-Option MonthCount.month → DbErr → 500 for all of /api/stats), STRFTIME('%Y-%m','2026')='-4707-06' (Julian-day misparse), and '2026-06' >= DATE('now','-12 months') passes lexically so the NULL row is reachable. validate.rs:43-49 explicitly accepts YYYY/YYYY-MM. P1 stands.

title: Document and provide a safe SQLite backup story (WAL-aware) before first release
description: There is zero backup guidance or tooling anywhere: no mention of 'backup' in README.md, deploy/kammerz.service, or src/ (grep verified). The DB runs in WAL mode (src/db.rs:52 'PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL'), so the obvious thing a self-hoster will do — cp /opt/kammerz/data/kammerz.db to a backup location while the server runs — is unsafe: recent commits live in kammerz.db-wal, and a mid-checkpoint copy of just the .db file can be stale or torn. For a catalog app whose entire value is years of irreplaceable shooting records, shipping a first release with no safe backup path is a data-loss footgun. Suggested fix, in increasing order of effort: (1) README 'Backups' section documenting sqlite3 /opt/kammerz/data/kammerz.db "VACUUM INTO '/path/backup-$(date +%F).db'" (or '.backup') as the only safe online method, plus a note that naive cp requires stopping the service first; (2) an authenticated GET /api/backup endpoint that runs VACUUM INTO a temp file and streams it — gives the phone-over-VPN user one-tap backups with no shell access to the NAS; (3) optional periodic VACUUM INTO from a tokio task. Related but distinct: kammerz-2e2 (full-catalog JSON export) is a portability feature, not a faithful DB backup (it would lose sessions/settings and any future tables); this bead is about the SQLite file itself.
notes: verifier: confirmed — grep finds zero 'backup' mentions in README.md, deploy/, src/; WAL is set at src/db.rs:52 so a live cp of just kammerz.db is stale/torn (misses -wal). Not a duplicate of kammerz-2e2 (P4 JSON export = portability, loses sessions/settings, not a faithful file backup). P1 stands; README VACUUM-INTO section is the minimum bar, the /api/backup endpoint can be follow-up.

title: Unquoted KAMMERZ_PASSWORD_HASH in .env is mangled by dotenvy $-substitution → silent total lockout
description: dotenvy (0.15.7, loaded at src/main.rs:23 'dotenvy::dotenv().ok()') performs $VAR substitution in UNQUOTED and double-quoted .env values (verified in dotenvy-0.15.7/src/parse.rs ~line 211: bare '$' enters SubstitutionMode::Block; undefined vars substitute to empty). An argon2 hash is full of '$' tokens ('$argon2id$v=19$m=19456,t=2,p=1$<salt>$<hash>'), so the documented config path — README.md:55-65 and .env.example both show 'KAMMERZ_PASSWORD_HASH=$argon2id$v=19$...' unquoted — yields a mangled value like '=19=19456,t=2,p=1' at runtime. Consequences: config.password_hash is Some(garbage) (src/config.rs:16 only filters empty strings), auth_enabled()==true, and verify_password (src/auth/password.rs:13-20) silently returns false because PasswordHash::new fails — every login gets 'incorrect password' with ZERO log signal. First-run user following the README exactly is locked out of their own app with no diagnostic. (systemd EnvironmentFile= doesn't substitute, so the deploy/kammerz.service path happens to work — which makes the dev/manual-run failure even more confusing.) Fix (belt and braces): (1) single-quote the hash in .env.example and README ('...'); strong-quote disables dotenvy substitution; (2) validate at startup — in main.rs after AppConfig::from_env(), if password_hash is Some but argon2::PasswordHash::new() fails, exit with a clear error ('KAMMERZ_PASSWORD_HASH is not a valid argon2 hash — did $-substitution in your .env mangle it? Single-quote the value.'); (3) optionally tracing::error! in verify_password when the hash fails to parse.
notes: verifier: confirmed empirically — built a minimal dotenvy-0.15.7 repro: unquoted KAMMERZ_PASSWORD_HASH=$argon2id$v=19$m=19456,t=2,p=1$<salt>$<hash> in .env loads as '=19=19456,t=2,p=1' (total mangle); single-quoting preserves it intact. verify_password returns false with zero log output. README.md:61 shows the unquoted form. systemd EnvironmentFile path is unaffected (no substitution), so only dev/manual runs lock out — P1 stands.

title: Roll detail page infinite data-fetch loop — page-load $effect tracks 'roll', which loadRollData rewrites
description:
Visiting any roll detail page (e.g. /rolls/1, /rolls/1?from=dashboard) triggers an unbounded loop that re-fetches /api/rolls/{id}/detail + the reference catalogs (cameras, film-stocks, lenses, lens-mounts, labs) forever — backend logs show the cameras→film-stocks→lenses→lens-mounts→labs→detail cycle repeating dozens of times per second with no end. The page still renders (loading flips false after the first resolve), but it hammers the server continuously (wasteful CPU/battery, and over the field VPN, data).

ROOT CAUSE (frontend/src/routes/(app)/rolls/[id]/+page.svelte): the page-load $effect (~line 822) is:
  $effect(() => { loading = true; Promise.all([loadRefData(), loadRollData()]).finally(() => { loading = false; }); });
It is meant to track ONLY 'id'. But Svelte 5 tracks every reactive READ during the effect's synchronous execution, INCLUDING the synchronous prefix of the async functions it calls. loadRollData()'s prefix (before the first await getRollDetail(id) at ~line 364) reads roll at ~line 359: 'const prevStatus = roll?.status'. So the effect now depends on 'roll'. After the await, ~line 365 'roll = detail.roll' writes a brand-new object into roll, invalidating the effect → it re-runs → reads roll → fetches → writes roll → loops. Because the write is ASYNC (post-await), Svelte's synchronous update-depth guard never fires, so it loops forever instead of throwing effect_update_depth_exceeded.

The ?from=dashboard query param is incidental — the effect never reads searchParams; the loop happens on every roll-detail entry regardless of source. Almost certainly introduced by the auto-status-notice feature (kammerz-9xg), which added the prevStatus = roll?.status snapshot inside loadRollData; before that, loadRollData read only the stable 'id', so the effect ran once.
design: The effect must depend on 'id' alone. Options: (a) read id explicitly at the top of the effect and wrap the loaders in untrack(): $effect(() => { void id; untrack(() => { loading = true; Promise.all([loadRefData(), loadRollData()]).finally(() => loading = false); }); }); — import untrack from 'svelte'. (b) Take the prevStatus snapshot via untrack(() => roll?.status) inside loadRollData so the read isn't tracked, OR pass prevStatus in from the caller. (a) is the most robust because it also shields any OTHER incidental reactive read in either loader's sync prefix from ever re-arming the effect. Add an e2e/Playwright assertion that loading /rolls/[id] issues each catalog request a bounded number of times (e.g. /api/rolls/1/detail fired exactly once within N ms) so a regression is caught — the smoke suite currently visits /rolls (list) but not the detail page, which is why this slipped through.
acceptance_criteria: Navigating to /rolls/[id] (with or without ?from=) fetches the roll detail and each reference catalog a bounded number of times (once on entry; again only on an actual id change or a user mutation). No runaway request logs. A regression test exercises the roll-detail route and asserts request counts stay bounded.

title: Enforce formatting: fix stale dprint config, run dprint+rustfmt repo-wide, add CI format job
description: dprint.jsonc exists (commit 3bd0b0b) but is stale from the Tauri era: includes cover src/**/*.{ts,js,svelte,css,json} (now the Rust tree) and root-level files only, so frontend/ and docs are never formatted; nothing runs dprint before commits and CI has no format job. cargo fmt --check also shows diffs across the workspace (no repo rustfmt.toml pins behavior). Surfaced reviewing PR #48's unformatted README changes. Fix: update dprint.jsonc for the axum+SvelteKit layout (cover frontend/, *.md docs, add toml+yaml plugins), add a repo rustfmt.toml, add just fmt / fmt-check recipes and hook fmt-check into just check, add a CI format job (dprint check + cargo fmt --check), run the repo-wide format, and document the run-before-commit expectation in CLAUDE.md.

title: Add backend tests for dev-record deletion status reverts and sibling/no-regression branches
description:
The documented delete-side transitions of the roll state machine have zero test coverage. tests/development.rs thoroughly covers create/update sync (create_lab_dev_advances_status:117, orphan advances:144/172, update add/clear date:229-360, scanned untouched on update-clear:370) but the only delete tests are the 404 cases (delete_missing_lab_dev_returns_404:408, delete_missing_self_dev_returns_404:423). Untested, spec-documented transitions (CLAUDE.md 'Data-driven status sync'):

1. delete_lab_dev: at-lab/lab-done -> shot revert (src/routes/development.rs:276-282)
2. delete_self_dev: developing/developed -> shot revert (development.rs:465-471)
3. Sibling-skip branches: delete one dev type while the other exists -> revert skipped (development.rs:269-275, 458-464) — currently only reachable via the kammerz-ysw hole, but it's live logic
4. delete dev record at 'scanned' -> stays 'scanned' (spec: 'status beyond a data type's range is not affected')
5. tests/shots.rs:98 covers delete-last-shot loaded revert, but not the no-op when the roll is past 'shot' (e.g. at-lab with zero shots stays at-lab)
6. create_lab_dev/create_self_dev on a roll at 'scanned' stays 'scanned' (forward-only no-op on the CREATE path — only the update path is tested at development.rs:370)

Why it matters for first release: the state machine is the user's #1 correctness concern, and exactly half of its transition table (the reverts) is unguarded against regression — these are the branches most likely to be touched again by kammerz-afc/kammerz-ysw follow-ups.

Suggested fix: add ~6 integration tests to tests/development.rs and tests/shots.rs mirroring the existing create_roll_at_status + roll_status helpers (tests/development.rs:13-44).
notes: verifier: confirmed — tests/development.rs test list verified: create/update sync covered (lines 117-401) but delete coverage is only delete_missing_*_returns_404 (:408,:423); the at-lab/lab-done→shot and developing/developed→shot reverts, sibling-skip branches, delete-at-scanned no-op, and create-at-scanned forward-only no-op have zero tests; tests/shots.rs:98 covers only the loaded revert, not the past-shot no-op. P2 calibrated correctly (test debt, not a live bug).

title: DevelopmentSection: dev-record delete failures are silent (rethrow into void handler)
description:
confirmDeleteDev catches and rethrows the API error into ConfirmDialog's synchronous onconfirm callback, producing an unhandled promise rejection with zero user-visible feedback. The confirm dialog has already hidden itself, so a failed delete just... does nothing.

Evidence:
- frontend/src/lib/components/rolls/DevelopmentSection.svelte:222-234 — 'catch (err) { throw err; }' is the only error path; no error state is set and showDevDeleteConfirm stays true on failure (while ConfirmDialog's internal open flag is already false via its handleConfirm at ConfirmDialog.svelte:32-35), leaving inconsistent state.
- ConfirmDialog.svelte:12 — onconfirm: () => void; nothing awaits or catches the rejected promise.
- Contrast with every other delete on these pages (e.g. rolls/[id]/+page.svelte:446-456 confirmDeleteShot, lenses/+page.svelte:237-247 confirmDelete) which set a visible error string.

Why it matters for first release: deleting a dev record is one of the status-sync triggers (lab dev deleted → at-lab/lab-done→shot). The backend friendly_err message (constraint problems, etc.) is exactly the kind of error the UI conventions say must surface; here it vanishes into the console.

Suggested fix: replace the rethrow with an error state rendered in the Development section (or pass it up via a prop/callback), and reset showDevDeleteConfirm in a finally block:
  catch (err) { devDeleteError = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err); }
notes: verifier: confirmed — confirmDeleteDev (:222-234) rethrows into ConfirmDialog's synchronous onconfirm (() => void, ConfirmDialog.svelte:12); handleConfirm (:32-35) neither awaits nor catches, so failures are unhandled rejections with no UI error. Worse than filed: DevelopmentSection renders <ConfirmDialog open={true}> unbound inside {#if showDevDeleteConfirm} (:451-459), so after a failed delete the dialog's internal open=false while showDevDeleteConfirm stays true — component stays mounted-hidden and the x delete buttons (:272, :299, which only set showDevDeleteConfirm=true) can never reopen it until navigation. P2 appropriate.
close_reason: duplicate of kammerz-2qk (same root cause, broader app-wide scope there)

title: Roll edit: switching to a fixed-lens camera saves a stale lens_id
description:
In roll detail edit mode, selecting a fixed-lens camera hides the Default Lens select and displays the camera's fixed lens — but saveEditRoll still submits the old editLensId, so the roll is persisted with camera=<fixed-lens camera> and lens_id=<previous camera's lens>.

Evidence (frontend/src/routes/(app)/rolls/[id]/+page.svelte):
- :604-615 startEditRoll seeds editLensId from roll.lens_id.
- :693-702 — when editIsFixedLens && editFixedLens, the lens Select is replaced by a read-only display of the fixed lens; editLensId is never updated.
- :617-629 saveEditRoll sends 'lens_id: editLensId ? Number(editLensId) : null' regardless.

Consequences: the roll's default lens stays a lens that physically cannot mount on the new camera. getShotLensDisplay (:470-475) then shows that wrong lens as '(roll default)' on shots with no per-shot override, and the rolls list shows the wrong lens column. The shot dialog's lens cascade happens to mask it (fixedLens wins at :326), making the bad data hard to notice. The inverse direction also leaves debris: switching AWAY from a fixed-lens camera keeps the old fixed lens as editLensId, visible but incompatible.

Note rolls/new gets this right via its auto-populate effect (frontend/src/routes/(app)/rolls/new/+page.svelte:194-201), which sets lensId from the camera's default_lens_id; the edit form has no equivalent.

Suggested fix: in saveEditRoll, when editIsFixedLens send editFixedLens?.id ?? null instead of editLensId; or mirror the rolls/new effect to re-seed editLensId when editCameraId changes.
notes: verifier: confirmed — editLensId is only written in startEditRoll (:609) and by the Default Lens Select binding (:701), which is hidden when editIsFixedLens (:693-699); saveEditRoll (:624) unconditionally submits editLensId, so switching to a fixed-lens camera persists the previous camera's lens as roll default. No $effect re-seeds editLensId on editCameraId change (verified by grep); rolls/new does have the equivalent effect (new/+page.svelte:193-201, including the else-clear). getShotLensDisplay fallback (:470-475) and the inverse switch-away debris also check out. P2 appropriate.

title: Failed delete leaves ConfirmDialog unre-openable on every page; DevelopmentSection swallows the error entirely
description:
Two interacting bugs around the delete-confirmation flow:

1) ConfirmDialog self-closes via an unbound $bindable prop, leaving parent state stuck. ConfirmDialog.svelte:32-35 sets 'open = false' BEFORE calling onconfirm. Most pages render it as '{#if deletingX}<ConfirmDialog open={true} ...>' (labs/+page.svelte:185-194, film-stocks/+page.svelte:293-300, lenses delete, rolls/[id]/+page.svelte:1031-1040 deletingShotId, DevelopmentSection.svelte:451-460). Because 'open' is passed as a literal (not bind:), the child's assignment only flips its local copy. If the async delete then FAILS, the parent's guard state (deletingLab / deletingStock / deletingShotId / showDevDeleteConfirm) stays truthy, so the {#if} block never unmounts-and-remounts; clicking the row's delete button again assigns the same value (same object/number → no reactive change) and the dialog — whose internal open is now false — never reappears. Net effect: after one failed delete, that delete button is dead until page reload.

2) DevelopmentSection additionally surfaces no error at all: DevelopmentSection.svelte:222-234 confirmDeleteDev's catch block is 'catch (err) { throw err; }' — the rejection escapes ConfirmDialog's fire-and-forget onconfirm call (ConfirmDialog.svelte:34) as an unhandled promise rejection. The dialog has already closed itself, so a failed lab/self dev deletion looks exactly like a success (record reappears only after a manual refresh... actually it never disappears, but there is zero feedback about why). All other pages at least write to the page-level 'error' banner.

Why it matters for first release: deletes CAN fail here (FK constraints, VPN drop — the app is used over flaky field connections), and the failure mode is silent + leaves dead UI.

Suggested fix: in ConfirmDialog, don't set open=false in handleConfirm — let the parent close it (or use bind:open everywhere and reset parent state in oncancel/onconfirm). In DevelopmentSection, replace 'throw err' with writing a visible error (e.g. page error via a new onerror callback or a local error banner) and reset showDevDeleteConfirm in a finally.
notes:
verifier: confirmed — ConfirmDialog.svelte:32-35 flips unbound bindable open=false pre-onconfirm; labs:90-100 and film-stocks:171-181 catch blocks leave the {#if} guard (deletingLab/deletingStock) truthy so the literal open={true} never re-propagates and the dialog is dead until reload; same pattern at rolls/[id]:1031 (deletingShotId) and DevelopmentSection:452. DevelopmentSection.svelte:222-234 is literally 'catch (err) { throw err; }' — silent unhandled rejection. Note rolls/[id]:1022 delete-roll uses bind:open correctly, proving the fix shape. P2 stands.
merged kammerz-b4y (frontend finder): DevelopmentSection rethrows delete errors into a void handler; its verifier additionally found the failed delete leaves ConfirmDialog mounted-but-closed (open={true} inside {#if}), permanently disabling dev deletion until navigation

title: Quick Entry: roll-change $effect clobbers manual lens selection after every save
description:
The roll-change $effect in Quick Entry depends on the rolls array, and handleSave reassigns rolls after each saved shot — so the effect re-runs and resets the lens dropdown to the roll/camera default, silently overriding the lens the photographer manually selected.

Evidence (frontend/src/routes/(app)/quick-entry/+page.svelte):
- :207-222 — $effect reads selectedRollId, rolls (via rolls.find at :212), and selectedCamera (derived from rolls+cameras), then sets selectedLensId = roll.lens_id || camera.default_lens_id.
- :172-175 — handleSave does '[shots, rolls] = await Promise.all([listShotsForRoll(...), listRolls()])', reassigning rolls.
- :179 — comment 'Keep selectedLensId — photographer usually uses the same lens per roll' states the opposite intent; the effect defeats it whenever the roll has a default lens (the common case).

Secondary issues from the same effect:
1. The re-run also calls loadRollData() (:210), refetching shots + suggestNextFrame that handleSave just fetched — duplicate requests per save, and the two suggestNextFrame calls can race.
2. When switching to a roll with NO lens_id and no camera default, selectedLensId keeps the previous roll's lens (no else-clause clearing it), so the next shot can be logged with a lens from a different camera system.

Why it matters for first release: Quick Entry is the in-the-field rapid-logging surface; shots silently recorded with the wrong lens are corrupted data the user won't notice until scanning.

Suggested fix: split the effect — track only selectedRollId (e.g. snapshot rolls via untrack(), or move the lens-default seeding into an onRollChange handler on the Select) so saving never re-seeds; explicitly clear selectedLensId when the new roll has no applicable default.
notes: verifier: confirmed — rolls is $state (:17); the $effect (:207-222) reads rolls via rolls.find (:212) and the rolls-derived selectedCamera, and handleSave reassigns rolls (:172-175), so every save re-runs the effect, re-seeding selectedLensId (defeats the 'Keep selectedLensId' intent at :179 whenever roll.lens_id or camera.default_lens_id is set). Duplicate loadRollData/suggestNextFrame per save and missing else-clear (stale lens carrying across rolls with no defaults) both verified — no else branch resets selectedLensId. P2 appropriate: silent wrong-lens metadata on the in-field surface.

title: Dialogs have no focus management: no initial focus, no trap, no restore — Escape often doesn't work
description:
Dialog.svelte and ConfirmDialog.svelte render role=dialog/alertdialog with aria-modal=true but do no focus management:

- frontend/src/lib/components/ui/Dialog.svelte:16-18,34 — the Escape handler is 'onkeydown' on the dialog wrapper div, so it only fires when focus is INSIDE the dialog. After opening a dialog by clicking a page button, focus remains on that trigger (outside the dialog), so pressing Escape does nothing until the user clicks/tabs into the dialog. Same pattern in ConfirmDialog.svelte:37-39,55.
- No element receives focus when the dialog opens (no autofocus / programmatic .focus()), so keyboard and screen-reader users are left positioned in the background page.
- No focus trap and no 'inert' on the rest of the app: Tab walks out of the 'modal' into the background page (which is also still scrollable), contradicting aria-modal=true.
- Focus is not restored to the trigger on close.
- Dialog.svelte:46-51 — the close button's only content is '×' with no aria-label; screen readers announce it as 'multiplication sign' or nothing useful.

Why it matters for first release: every create/edit flow in the app goes through these two components (shot dialog, dev dialogs, all list-page add/edit dialogs, ConfirmDialog for every destructive action), so the keyboard/AT experience of the whole app hinges on them. The broken-Escape case also bites mouse users.

Suggested fix in the shared components (fixes every dialog at once): on open, save document.activeElement, focus the dialog panel (tabindex='-1') or first focusable; listen for Escape on window (svelte:window) while open; restore focus on close; add a simple Tab/Shift-Tab wrap handler or set 'inert' on the app root while a dialog is open; add aria-label='Close' to the × button.
notes: verifier: confirmed — Dialog.svelte:16-18,34 and ConfirmDialog.svelte:37-39,55 attach Escape to the wrapper div (inert until focus enters the dialog); no initial focus, no trap, no restore, no aria-label on the × button; a11y lint suppressed via svelte-ignore. Fix in the two shared components covers every dialog. P2 stands.

title: Chevron click into a dev status neither honors nor explains the clicked target (Lab Done click can land at At Lab)
description: handleStatusClick (frontend/src/routes/(app)/rolls/[id]/+page.svelte:542-573) intercepts a forward click into at-lab/lab-done/developing/developed when no dev record exists and opens the generic dev dialog instead (devAutoPrompt). Three UX gaps, all verified: (1) Target downgrade — clicking 'Lab Done' opens the Lab Development dialog, which pre-fills only date_dropped_off (DevelopmentSection.svelte:126); if the user saves without Date Received, the backend lands the roll at at-lab, not the lab-done they clicked (src/services/roll_service.rs:282-292, date_received drives the target). Nothing in the dialog says which field decides where they land. (2) No explanation — the dialog gives no hint WHY it appeared instead of the status just changing, or that saving will change status at all. (3) Silent abort — Cancel closes the dialog and the status click is silently dropped; the chevron bar looks untouched with no message. Why it matters: this is the single most-travelled transition in the lab path and it routinely lands somewhere other than where the user clicked. Suggested fix: pass the clicked target into DevelopmentSection (e.g. autoPrompt: {kind:'lab', target:'lab-done'}); when target is lab-done/developed, seed date_received/date_processed with today; add a one-line contextual note in the dialog ('Saving will move this roll to At Lab — set Date Received to mark it Lab Done'); optionally show a brief inline notice when the dialog is cancelled ('Status unchanged').
notes: verifier: confirmed — handleStatusClick (rolls/[id]/+page.svelte:554-562) collapses a Lab Done/Developed click into devAutoPrompt='lab'|'self' with no target; DevelopmentSection autoPrompt effect (lines 78-86) just opens the dialog, openLabDevDialog seeds only date_dropped_off (line 126), and sync_lab_dev_status (roll_service.rs:282-293) targets AtLab when has_received=false, so saving lands one rung short of the clicked chevron with no in-dialog explanation; Cancel silently drops the click. P2 is right — most-travelled lab-path transition, though self-healing later (forward-only resync advances once date_received is filled).

title: Hover-only (opacity-0 group-hover) edit/delete controls are invisible on touch devices and keyboard focus
description:
Row/card action buttons across the app are hidden with 'opacity-0 transition-opacity group-hover:opacity-100', so they are invisible until a mouse hovers the row. On a phone (the primary field device) there is no hover: iOS sometimes emulates it on first tap, but that is undiscoverable and unreliable, and the controls are completely invisible to keyboard users too (the buttons remain in tab order but stay at opacity-0 when focused — no focus-within reveal).

Occurrences (all verified):
- frontend/src/routes/(app)/rolls/[id]/+page.svelte:963 — per-shot Edit / delete buttons
- frontend/src/lib/components/rolls/DevelopmentSection.svelte:270,297 — lab/self dev record Edit / delete
- frontend/src/routes/(app)/labs/+page.svelte:141 — lab Edit / delete
- frontend/src/routes/(app)/film-stocks/+page.svelte:259 — film stock delete
- frontend/src/routes/(app)/cameras/[id]/+page.svelte:529 — unlink-lens button
- frontend/src/routes/(app)/lenses/+page.svelte:345 — lens Edit / delete
- frontend/src/lib/components/rolls/RollTimeline.svelte:55 — pencil icon (cosmetic only; the button itself is visible)

Why it matters for first release: editing/deleting a shot from a phone in the field is a core workflow (rolls/[id] shots list), and the user can't see that the controls exist. Invisible-but-focusable buttons are also an a11y failure (WCAG 2.4.7 focus visible).

Suggested fix: add 'focus-within:opacity-100' to the hidden containers, and make them always visible where hover is unavailable — e.g. a 'pointer-coarse:opacity-100' variant (Tailwind 4 supports pointer-coarse) or '@media (hover: none) { opacity: 1 }'. Many of these '×' delete buttons are also tiny (size=sm ghost Button, ~28px); bump touch target to >=40px while at it.
notes:
verifier: confirmed — grep verifies all 6 cited action-button sites use opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 with no focus-within or pointer-coarse fallback (rolls/[id]:963, DevelopmentSection:270,297, labs:141, film-stocks:259, cameras/[id]:529, lenses:345). Distinct from kammerz-c6o (layout vs interaction affordance). P2 calibrated.
merged kammerz-t34 (workflow-ux finder, independently found): same opacity-0 group-hover controls on shot rows and dev records

title: Hover-revealed Edit/Delete controls are invisible on touch devices (shots, dev records)
description: Shot rows and development-record cards hide their Edit and Delete buttons behind 'opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100': frontend/src/routes/(app)/rolls/[id]/+page.svelte:963 (shot row actions) and frontend/src/lib/components/rolls/DevelopmentSection.svelte:270,297 (lab/self dev card actions). On a phone (the stated field-use device) there is no hover: the buttons are invisible until a tap happens to trigger emulated hover (unreliable, varies by mobile browser), so editing a shot or a development record is undiscoverable on touch. The buttons remain tappable while fully transparent, which also invites accidental taps. Why it matters: logging/correcting shots from the field is the core loop. Suggested fix: use a media-query approach — keep hover reveal on pointer-fine devices but always show the controls (or a kebab menu) on coarse pointers, e.g. Tailwind 'opacity-100 pointer-fine:opacity-0 pointer-fine:group-hover:opacity-100' via a media variant, or '@media (hover: none) { opacity: 1 }'.
notes: verifier: confirmed — opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 wraps the only Edit/Delete affordances for shot rows (rolls/[id]/+page.svelte:963-966) and lab/self dev cards (DevelopmentSection.svelte:270,297); no @media (hover:none) or pointer-coarse variant anywhere in frontend/src. Invisible-but-tappable buttons on the stated field device; P2 stands (subsumed in practice by the broader mobile pass in kammerz-xys but worth its own fix).

title: Add integration tests for untested compound endpoints (camera with-lens, link/unlink, maintenance, roll delete, import roll)
description: Several routes have ZERO integration tests, including the most complex transactional handlers: (1) POST /api/cameras/with-lens — the 4-step transactional fixed-lens create (camera -> lens -> junction -> default_lens_id) at src/routes/cameras.rs:237-316; this is the headline structural invariant in CLAUDE.md (fixed-lens cameras MUST be created via this path) and nothing exercises it. (2) POST/DELETE /api/cameras/{id}/lenses/{lens_id} link/unlink (cameras.rs:351-371) and GET /{id}/lenses. (3) The entire camera-maintenance CRUD sub-router (cameras.rs:108-114, 375+). (4) PUT/DELETE /api/cameras/{id} and PUT/DELETE /api/lenses/{id} — tests/cameras.rs (53 lines) and tests/lenses.rs (48 lines) cover only list+create+get. (5) DELETE /api/rolls/{id} (FK cascade of shots/dev records) and the happy-path PUT /api/rolls/{id} (only the malformed-date 422 is tested, tests/rolls.rs:190-198). (6) POST /api/import/roll — the transactional roll+shots+lens-links import (src/routes/import.rs:64, RollService::import_roll at src/services/roll_service.rs:171-215); tests/import.rs (21 lines) only tests the missing-API-key 422 on /models, and /api/import/roll needs no API key so it is fully testable. Why it matters: these are exactly the multi-statement transactions where a partial-failure or DTO regression loses or corrupts data, and CI cannot catch it. Suggested fix: add integration tests per endpoint group following the existing tests/common/mod.rs open_app() pattern; for with-lens, assert camera+lens+junction+default_lens_id all persisted.
notes: verifier: confirmed — tests/cameras.rs (53 lines) covers only list/create/get; no test touches POST /api/cameras/with-lens (4-step txn, cameras.rs:237-316), link/unlink (:351-371), maintenance CRUD, any PUT/DELETE on cameras/lenses/rolls, or POST /api/import/roll (which needs no API key — import.rs:117 never calls resolve_key — so it is fully testable; tests/import.rs only asserts the /models 422). P2 calibrated. Distinct from kammerz-8rh (dev-deletion sync tests).

title: Add integration tests for dev-record deletion status reverts and sibling-dev guard
description: The status-sync transitions for DEV RECORD DELETION have no test coverage, while every create/update transition is well tested. Untested transitions: (1) lab dev deleted -> at-lab/lab-done -> shot (src/routes/development.rs:250-292), (2) self dev deleted -> developing/developed -> shot (src/routes/development.rs:439-481), (3) the sibling-dev priority guard that skips the revert when the other dev record still exists (development.rs:269-283 and 458-472). Also: shot-deletion revert is only tested for shooting->loaded (tests/shots.rs:98-127); the shot->loaded variant of the from-set and the no-revert case (deleting one of several shots) are untested. Why it matters: these are transactional handlers that silently mutate roll status — a regression corrupts catalog state with no failing test. tests/development.rs (434 lines) covers create/update sync thoroughly but only the delete-of-missing-404 case for deletes. Suggested fix: add ~6 integration tests in tests/development.rs and tests/shots.rs mirroring the existing create/update sync tests (create dev at status X, DELETE it, assert roll status; repeat with a sibling dev present and assert status untouched).
close_reason: verifier: duplicate of kammerz-8rh, which covers the same dev-deletion revert + sibling-guard test gap (development.rs:250-292/439-481) plus extra no-op cases (scanned-untouched, create-path forward-only). Finding itself is factually correct.

title: Create a release pipeline: tagged Linux builds for the NAS + surface the version
description: There is no release packaging at all: no git tags, no GitHub releases, and .github/workflows/ contains only ci.yml (build/test/e2e — verified). README.md:67-78 documents deployment as 'sudo install target/release/kammerz' on the server, but development happens on macOS (this repo) and the target is a Linux NAS — 'just build' here produces a Mach-O arm64 binary that cannot run there, and neither README nor justfile mentions cross-compilation or building on the server. The version (Cargo.toml:6, 0.1.0) is also never surfaced: not in the startup log (src/main.rs:95), not in /api/health (src/routes/mod.rs:62-64), so once deployed you cannot tell which build a NAS is running. For a first release: (1) add a tag-triggered release.yml workflow that builds x86_64-unknown-linux-musl and aarch64-unknown-linux-musl (cargo-zigbuild or cross; musl+rustls means a fully static binary, and the SPA is already embedded — single-artifact deploy) after building the frontend, and attaches the binaries to a GitHub release; (2) log env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION") at startup and include it in /api/health (pairs with kammerz health-depth bead); (3) update README deployment section to point at the release artifact (or document the cross-compile command) instead of an implicit on-server build.
notes: Reworked per review: original tag-triggered Actions release pipeline dropped (Actions billing-dead; user wants AI-authored release notes; no pip deps). New scope: version surfacing (kept) + just build-linux (aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu via messense brew toolchain, .cargo/config.toml linker) + just deploy <user>@<host> (fewd-pattern ssh deploy with /api/health version verification). Release = deploy to the DietPi; no GH releases. PR #44 force-pushed + retitled.

title: Add graceful shutdown (SIGTERM/SIGINT) to the axum server
description: src/main.rs:99-104 calls axum::serve(...) with no .with_graceful_shutdown(), so the default SIGTERM disposition kills the process instantly. deploy/kammerz.service relies on systemd's stop (SIGTERM), so every 'systemctl restart kammerz' (i.e., every upgrade) hard-kills in-flight requests: a write request mid-transaction is aborted and the client gets a connection reset instead of a response; session writes can be dropped. SQLite/WAL makes this crash-safe (no corruption), but it's still avoidable request loss on the single most common operational action, and it leaves the WAL un-checkpointed — which compounds the 'naive file copy backup' hazard (kammerz-u29) because kammerz.db alone is then guaranteed stale. Suggested fix: add the standard shutdown_signal() helper (tokio::signal::ctrl_c + unix SIGTERM via tokio::signal::unix::signal), pass it to axum::serve(...).with_graceful_shutdown(...), and after serve returns, optionally run PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE) and close the pools, then log 'shutdown complete'. ~15 lines, no new deps (tokio 'full' is already enabled, Cargo.toml:23).
notes: verifier: confirmed — axum::serve at main.rs:99-104 has no .with_graceful_shutdown, and deploy/kammerz.service uses default SIGTERM stop; tokio 'full' (Cargo.toml:23) already provides the signal feature. Calibration note: in-flight request loss is marginal for single-user (the restarter IS the user), the real value is clean pool close → WAL checkpoint so a stopped-service copy of kammerz.db alone is complete, which compounds with kammerz-u29. P2 stands given the ~15-line cost.

title: Snapshot the DB before running pending startup migrations
description: Migrations run unconditionally and automatically at every server start (src/db.rs:54 'Migrator::up(&db, None).await?' inside init(), called from src/main.rs:52) with foreign_keys=OFF and no pre-migration backup. The project has already documented (CLAUDE.md, 'Migration raw SQL gotcha') that execute_unprepared() auto-commits per statement, so a migration failing midway leaves partial data persisted while the migration is NOT recorded in seaql_migrations — it re-runs on next start and can create duplicates that need manual sqlite3 surgery; migration 020 exists precisely because a past migration destroyed data. For a self-hoster, 'upgrade the binary, restart the service' is the normal flow, and right now that flow mutates the only copy of the catalog with no rollback point. Suggested fix in db::init(): before Migrator::up, check Migrator::get_pending_migrations(&db); if non-empty and the DB is file-backed (skip for sqlite::memory:), run VACUUM INTO '<db-path>.pre-migrate-<timestamp>' (or std::fs::copy before the pool opens, since nothing else has written yet) and log the snapshot path at INFO. Optionally prune snapshots older than N. Cheap insurance that turns a botched future migration from data loss into a rename.
notes: verifier: confirmed — db.rs:54 runs Migrator::up unconditionally with foreign_keys=OFF (line 53) and no snapshot; migration/src/m20260223_000020_repair_fixed_lens_data.rs is concrete evidence a past migration destroyed data, and CLAUDE.md documents the execute_unprepared per-statement auto-commit re-run/duplicate hazard. One caveat to the suggested fix: fs::copy before the pool opens must also copy -wal/-shm if a prior crash left an un-checkpointed WAL — VACUUM INTO via the open connection is the safer variant. P2 calibrated.

title: Commit bun.lock and install frozen in CI — pin the frontend supply chain baked into the release binary
description: bun.lock is gitignored (top-level .gitignore 'Bun' section) and CI explicitly installs fresh: .github/workflows/ci.yml frontend job comment 'No lockfile is committed (bun.lock is gitignored), so install fresh' followed by bare 'bun install'. The e2e job does the same and then embeds whatever frontend/build it produced into the release binary via rust-embed. Why it matters for a first release: every CI run and every release build resolves npm semver ranges at build time — a compromised or merely broken minor release of any transitive dependency flows straight into the binary the user runs, unreviewed and unreproducible (you can't even tell after the fact which versions shipped). The backend already does this right (Cargo.lock committed, cargo build/test --locked). Suggested fix: remove bun.lock from .gitignore, commit it, and switch CI installs to 'bun install --frozen-lockfile'. Optionally (same hygiene family): add a scheduled 'cargo audit' / 'bun audit' job so known-vuln advisories in pinned deps surface.
notes:
verifier: confirmed — .gitignore:38-39 ignores bun.lock; ci.yml frontend job (lines 52-53) and e2e job (line 76) run bare 'bun install' while the backend uses cargo --locked (lines 36-37, 80); the e2e job's release build embeds the unpinned resolution via rust-embed. frontend/bun.lock already exists locally, so the fix is commit + --frozen-lockfile. P2 appropriate: real supply-chain/reproducibility gap, trivial fix, but no evidence of active exploitation path for a self-built single-user binary.
merged kammerz-0x5 (devops finder, independently found): same fix — commit bun.lock + frozen install; also motivated by build reproducibility (cargo side is already --locked)

title: Stop returning the Anthropic API key in cleartext from the settings API
description: GET /api/settings/{key} (src/routes/settings.rs:22-28) returns any setting value verbatim, including claude_api_key — a billable credential. The import page (frontend/src/routes/(app)/import/+page.svelte:153-161) fetches the raw key into browser state and displays it in the settings form. Two problems for a first release: (1) in OPEN (no KAMMERZ_PASSWORD_HASH) mode, RequireAuth passes everyone (src/auth/middleware.rs:46-48), so any device on the LAN can silently exfiltrate the key with one unauthenticated GET — 'LAN-trust' for a film catalog is a very different bar than for a billing credential; (2) even with auth enabled, round-tripping the secret to the browser makes it exposable via any future XSS/extension/shared-device leak, with zero functional need — the UI only needs to know whether a key is saved. Suggested fix: treat claude_api_key as write-only. In get_setting, deny-list secret keys (return null, or a masked sentinel / {set: true} shape); keep PUT working; backend continues reading the real value via SettingsService::get_setting in resolve_key (src/routes/import.rs:71-84). Frontend: keySaved comes from the masked response; show 'Key saved — enter a new key to replace' instead of the stored value.
notes: verifier: confirmed — get_setting (src/routes/settings.rs:22-28) returns claude_api_key verbatim, RequireAuth is a no-op in open mode (src/auth/middleware.rs:46-48), and import/+page.svelte:150-171 round-trips the raw key into browser form state. Backend only needs the value server-side (resolve_key, src/routes/import.rs:71-84), so write-only GET is zero-loss. P2 calibration is right: real credential exposure, but bounded to the user's own LAN/VPN and avoidable by setting KAMMERZ_PASSWORD_HASH.

title: Set up GitHub Actions CI (backend tests, frontend check/build, e2e)
description:
There's no CI: every PR's quality gates (cargo test, bun run check, bun run build, Playwright e2e) are run manually/locally, and Copilot reviews but doesn't run tests. Add a GitHub Actions workflow so the gates run automatically on PRs and pushes to main.

Proposed jobs (.github/workflows/ci.yml):
- backend: cargo build + cargo test -p kammerz (in-memory SQLite, real migrations + seed). Cache the cargo registry + target dir.
- frontend: bun install (oven-sh/setup-bun), bun run check (svelte-check), bun run build (adapter-static). Cache bun + node_modules.
- e2e (optional, can be a follow-up): bun run test:e2e (Playwright smoke.spec.ts) against the built binary on :3002 — needs KAMMERZ_PASSWORD_HASH set and E2E_PASSWORD matching (the smoke test logs in via the real form). Install Playwright browsers.

Gotchas to account for:
- main.rs embeds frontend/build via rust-embed (#[folder = "frontend/build"]). The committed frontend/build/.gitkeep keeps the dir present so a plain cargo build/test compiles against an empty embed (fine for tests, which don't serve the SPA). For a release/e2e job, build the frontend first so the binary serves real assets.
- Use 'just' or call the underlying commands directly; the repo has a justfile (just check / just build) that already chains these.

Acceptance: a CI workflow that runs on pull_request + push to main, green on the current main; backend + frontend gates required; e2e at least wired (may start as non-blocking).
acceptance_criteria: GitHub Actions workflow runs cargo test + bun run check + bun run build on PRs and pushes to main; passes on current main; documented in CLAUDE.md commands or README if appropriate.
notes: Raised 2026-06-04 after a run of PRs (#21-#23) whose gates were all verified manually. justfile already has 'just check' (bun run check + cargo build + cargo test) and 'just build'.
close_reason: Completed in PR #25 — CI workflow (backend/frontend/e2e) merged to main

title: Lifecycle date capture & editing: confirm-on-transition + editable Timeline
description:
The roll-detail Timeline shows 'Received back' (lab-done) with no date, and lifecycle dates can only be set to 'today' (silent auto-stamp) or edited via a separate form — but the day a milestone happened is often not the day the user clicks the status chevron. Make date capture consistent and editable.

Decisions (brainstormed 2026-06-03):
- Paradigm: confirm-on-transition + inline-editable Timeline.
- Confirm prompt fires on FORWARD advance into a date-bearing, still-empty status: Shot (folds in the existing 'roll full' nudge), Lab Done (->date_received, the gap), Developed, Scanned, Post-processed, Archived. Prompt = date (default today) with Cancel/Confirm. NO 'Skip' (advance-but-blank) path — dropped as a smell (see kammerz-a7e). At Lab / Developing keep auto-opening their full dialogs (already capture dates). Backward moves keep ConfirmDialog, untouched.
- Timeline becomes the single home for lifecycle dates: each milestone row inline-editable (set/clear/cancel), routing saves to roll/lab/self update endpoints. Remove the 5 DateInputs from the roll Edit form (keep fuzzy date + non-date fields).
- Editing a date never changes status (status sync keys off shots/dev-record existence, not dates).
- No backend/schema changes — all target fields + update endpoints already exist. Frontend-only.

Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-03-lifecycle-date-capture-design.md
acceptance_criteria: Lab Done captures date_received on transition; all date-bearing forward transitions prompt an editable date (default today, Cancel/Confirm); Timeline rows are inline-editable and route to the correct record; roll Edit form no longer has date pickers; editing a date does not change status; cargo test + bun run check/build green; browser-verified lab-path walk with a back-dated Lab Done.
notes: Related: kammerz-a7e (state-machine path review). Independent of kammerz-r35 (film identity, PR #21) though both touch rolls/[id]/+page.svelte.

title: Move frontend dev server off the default Vite port (collides with fewd)
description:
The Kammerz frontend dev server runs on Vite's default :5173 (and the axum backend on :3002). The sibling fewd app (../fewd) is also configured to run on :5173 for dev, so the two collide when run at the same time. Pick a distinct, stable port for Kammerz dev and wire it through everywhere.

Touch points to update consistently:
- frontend/vite.config.ts — server.port (and the strictPort if set), plus the /api proxy target if the axum port also changes.
- justfile — the 'dev' / 'dev-frontend' recipes and any printed URLs.
- CLAUDE.md / UI_DESIGN docs that reference http://localhost:5173 and :3002.
- Any Playwright/verification helpers that hardcode :5173.

Consider also whether the axum backend :3002 needs to move (check fewd's backend port). Goal: kammerz dev and fewd dev can run simultaneously without a port clash.
acceptance_criteria: kammerz 'just dev' runs on a non-5173 frontend port with the /api proxy intact; fewd and kammerz dev can run at the same time; all docs/recipes reference the new port.
close_reason: Shipped in PR #18 (merged): Vite dev server moved to :5273 with strictPort; backend stays :3002; docs/recipes updated. kammerz and fewd dev can now run simultaneously.

title: Track & surface the full roll-lifecycle dates (rename "finished"; add scanned/received/post-process/archived)
description:
Follow-up to kammerz-a89. Clicking "shot" now stamps roll.date_finished, but "finished" is the wrong word and the wrong mental model: shooting is just the FIRST milestone. A roll is only truly finished after it moves through the rest of the pipeline, and every one of those transitions is a significant date the catalog should record and show.

PROBLEM
- Terminology: the UI calls date_finished "Finished" (roll detail header: "Finished {date}"). It means "finished shooting" — the roll is far from finished at that point. Relabel to "Finished shooting" (or "Shot") everywhere it surfaces.
- Missing significant dates: several lifecycle events have no date captured/visible.

CURRENT date columns (schema audit):
- roll: date_loaded, date_finished (= finished shooting), date_fuzzy
- development_lab: date_dropped_off, date_received
- development_self: date_processed
Gaps with NO date today: scanned, post-processed, archived. (developed/received/dropped-off exist on dev records but are not surfaced as a coherent timeline.)

DESIRED lifecycle dates (each significant, each visible in the UI):
1. Loaded (have: date_loaded)
2. Finished shooting (have: date_finished — relabel)
3. Developed — self: date_processed; lab: implied by lab turnaround
4. Received negatives back (lab path only) (have: development_lab.date_received)
5. Scanned (if the lab did not scan) — NO field yet
6. Post-processed — NO field yet
7. Archived — NO field yet
design:
Two parts, can be split into child issues:
(a) Terminology-only, cheap: relabel date_finished → "Finished shooting"/"Shot" in roll detail header and anywhere else it renders. Keep the DB column name date_finished (a rename migration is not worth the churn; the label is the user-facing concern). The nudge already says "Finished shooting".
(b) New dates + timeline UI: add the missing columns (scanned_at, post_processed_at, archived_at — names TBD) via migration; decide whether they live on the roll or are derived/attached to status transitions. Consider auto-stamping on the relevant status change (mirroring how date_finished is stamped on shooting→shot), with an editable override, since most are tied to a status move (scanned→scanned, archived→archived). Then surface ALL lifecycle dates as a single ordered "roll timeline" in the UI rather than scattering them across the header and dev cards. Cross-reference the status flow definitions in src/lib/utils/status.ts (labFlow/selfFlow) — the set of relevant dates is path-dependent (lab path has "received back"; self path does not).
acceptance_criteria:
- "Finished" no longer shown as a standalone label for date_finished; reads "Finished shooting" (or "Shot") wherever it surfaces.
- Each lifecycle milestone reachable by a roll (loaded, finished shooting, developed, received-back [lab only], scanned, post-processed, archived) has an associated date that is captured and visible in the UI.
- New milestone dates auto-stamp on their status transition (editable), consistent with the date_finished pattern, and never invent a date on a backward correction.
- Lifecycle dates presented as a coherent ordered timeline, path-aware (lab vs self).
close_reason: Shipped in PR #19 (merged): full roll-lifecycle dates + post-processed status + path-aware timeline, incl. self-review fixes and Copilot follow-up.

title: Replace dead undecided status chevrons with a development-path decision chevron
description: The undecided flow renders two non-interactive '·' placeholder divs between Shot and Scanned, while the real lab/self choice lives in a disconnected Development section (+Lab/+Self). Replace the dead dots with ONE actionable accent-tinted 'Develop ⌄' chevron that opens a 2-item popover (Lab / Self-Home). Choosing a path sets devAutoPrompt, opening the corresponding dev dialog; on save the backend auto-syncs status and the bar re-renders to labFlow/selfFlow via existing getFlowForPath. Scanned/Archived stay visible but muted as path-agnostic endpoints.
acceptance_criteria: Undecided bar shows a single live 'Develop' chevron (no dead dots); clicking opens Lab/Self menu; choosing opens the right dev dialog; after creating a dev record the bar re-renders to the chosen path's flow; chevron is a real button with aria-haspopup/expanded
close_reason: Shipped in PR #16 (merged): shot-date capture + development-path chevron, incl. code-review and Copilot follow-up fixes.

title: Capture shooting→shot transition date via Mark-as-Shot nudge
description: When a roll transitions to 'shot', date_finished is silently dropped — updateStatus('shot') writes only status. The date_finished field already exists end-to-end but is manual-only (Edit dialog). Add an editable date field (prefilled with today) to the existing 'Roll complete → Mark as Shot' nudge so the finish date is captured + confirmable in one gesture. Also stamp date_finished=today when entering 'shot' via the chevron if still empty, so the date is never lost.
acceptance_criteria: Nudge shows a date field prefilled with today; Mark as Shot writes date_finished; chevron-driven shot transition stamps today if empty; existing 'Finished {date}' header reflects it
close_reason: Shipped in PR #16 (merged): shot-date capture + development-path chevron, incl. code-review and Copilot follow-up fixes.

title: Fix pre-existing svelte-check type errors (30)
description: The frontend had 31 svelte-check errors + 1 warning before the port (present on the original main). Mostly 'type never' inference in search/stats and enum-string narrowing in film-stocks/rolls/import, plus process global in vite.config. Not caused by the port. Clean these up so 'bun run check' / CI is green.
notes:
Fixed all 30 svelte-check errors. Three root-cause categories:

1. 'never' inference (search/stats): `let x: T | null = $state(null)` puts the annotation on the variable but the rune's own generic infers `null`, so the null-guard narrowed to `never`. Fixed with `$state<T | null>(null)`.

2. Enum-string narrowing (cameras, film-stocks, import, rolls): <Select> binds string state (DOM reality — Select.value is $bindable('')), but DTO fields want enums (CameraFormat/CameraType/MaintenanceType/FilmFormat/FilmStockType/RollStatus/PushPull). Cast at the one-way API boundary (`x as Enum`); backend DeriveActiveEnum is the runtime validator.

3. Closure narrowing loss + ordering (rolls/[id], cameras/[id]): `roll?.x` guard narrowing is discarded inside nested .find() arrows on a reassignable let — re-apply `?.` inside the callback. Also moved frameProgress $derived above its use, and `x = r ?? undefined` for getRoll/getCamera returning T|null into a T|undefined state.

4. DevStageInsert mismatch (DevelopmentSection): nested stages in a self-dev create/update don't carry development_self_id (assigned server-side; backend StageDto omits it). Added NestedStageInsert = Omit<DevStageInsert,'development_self_id'> in api/development.ts.

Verified: bun run check = 0 errors / 0 warnings; bun run build succeeds.
close_reason: All 30 svelte-check errors fixed; bun run check and bun run build both green.

title: Invert CI definition: ci.yml jobs should call the just ci-* recipes
description: PR #55 created 'just ci' as a hand-copied mirror of .github/workflows/ci.yml — the pipeline is now defined twice with no sync mechanism (review finding: a future change to one silently diverges the other; drift already existed within the PR itself). When Actions is usable again, invert the dependency: install just in each job (taiki-e/install-action@just) and have ci.yml run 'just ci-backend' / 'just ci-frontend' / 'just e2e', keeping only Actions-specific concerns (caching, artifact upload, BUN_VERSION pin) in YAML — the justfile becomes the single source of truth.

title: Upgrade Rust workspace to edition 2024
description: The workspace crates (kammerz, entity, migration) are on edition = "2021" (Cargo.toml). Upgrade to edition 2024: bump edition in all three Cargo.toml files (and rust-version if needed), run cargo fix --edition, address any breakage (notably the new match ergonomics / RPIT capture rules and unsafe_op_in_unsafe_fn), and update rustfmt.toml's edition pin (added by kammerz-ai5) to 2024. Verify with just check.

title: Client-side required-field validation gaps on edit forms (camera edit, labs, roll ID)
description:
CLAUDE.md convention: 'Always validate required fields client-side before API calls (brand, model, mount, etc.).' The ADD dialogs follow it, but several edit/save paths don't, letting empty strings through to NOT-NULL TEXT columns (SQLite happily stores '').

Gaps verified:
- frontend/src/routes/(app)/cameras/[id]/+page.svelte:199-220 saveEdit — no check that editBrand/editModel are non-empty (the add path validates both at cameras/+page.svelte:188-190). Clearing Brand and saving produces a camera named ' XD-7' that breaks buildCameraLabels grouping and search.
- frontend/src/routes/(app)/labs/+page.svelte:42-58 handleAdd and :68-84 handleEdit — no name validation; a blank-name lab renders as an empty card and an empty option label in DevelopmentSection's lab dropdown.
- frontend/src/routes/(app)/rolls/new/+page.svelte:163-187 handleSubmit and rolls/[id]/+page.svelte:617-635 saveEditRoll — roll_id can be cleared to '' (the suggested ID is editable). roll_id is the roll's display identity everywhere (lists, search, dashboard, quick-entry labels), so an empty one yields anonymous '(shot)' rows.

Why it matters for first release: these are the only data-quality guards — the backend create/update DTOs trim but accept empty strings, and the convention explicitly says not to rely on opaque DB constraint errors.

Suggested fix: mirror the add-dialog pattern: early-return with the inline error string ('Brand is required.', 'Lab name is required.', 'Roll ID is required.') before each create/update call; also pass .trim()'d values in the payloads (several handlers currently send untrimmed brand/model/name).
notes: verifier: confirmed — camera saveEdit (cameras/[id]/+page.svelte:199-220) sends editBrand/editModel with no empty check while the add path validates (cameras/+page.svelte:187-190); labs handleAdd (:42-58) and handleEdit (:68-84) never validate name; rolls/new handleSubmit (:163-187) and rolls/[id] saveEditRoll (:617-635) submit roll_id unchecked. Backend confirms the gap is real: src/routes/rolls.rs uses trim(data.roll_id) (:124, :179) which passes '' through to the NOT-NULL TEXT column. P3 appropriate — single-user data-quality polish, requires deliberately clearing a field.

title: Quick Entry roll-full flow skips finish date and shows raw status keys
description: Two lifecycle inconsistencies on the Quick Entry page vs the roll detail page: (1) markRollShot (frontend/src/routes/(app)/quick-entry/+page.svelte:96-105) does updateRoll({status:'shot'}) with no date_finished, while the equivalent roll-detail nudge collects a 'Finished shooting' date and writes it with the status (rolls/[id]/+page.svelte:896-917, updateStatus 'shot'+finishDate). Finishing a roll from Quick Entry — the likely place to finish it, since that is where shots are logged — leaves the Timeline's 'Finished shooting' milestone permanently blank unless backfilled. (2) The roll picker labels embed the raw status key, label: roll_id + ' (' + r.status + ')' (quick-entry/+page.svelte:47,54), showing 'at-lab'/'post-processed' instead of the statusConfig labels ('At Lab', 'Post-processed') used everywhere else, violating the single-source-of-truth rule in status.ts. Suggested fix: reuse the roll-detail nudge pattern (DateInput seeded with last shot date/today + Mark as Shot) in Quick Entry, and use getStatusLabel(r.status) for the dropdown labels.
notes: verifier: confirmed — quick-entry markRollShot (lines 96-105) sends only {status:'shot'} with no date_finished while the roll-detail nudge (rolls/[id]/+page.svelte:896-917) collects a finish date and passes it to updateStatus('shot', finishDate); roll picker options (quick-entry lines 47,54) interpolate raw r.status keys instead of statusConfig labels, contrary to the status.ts single-source rule. P3 calibrated — consistency polish, Timeline date is backfillable.

title: suggest_id collides with existing roll_id after a same-day roll is deleted (COUNT-based suffix)
description:
RollService::suggest_id (src/services/roll_service.rs:356-376) computes the next YYMMDD-N suffix as COUNT(*) WHERE roll_id LIKE 'YYMMDD-%' + 1. Deleting any roll created the same day shrinks the count, so the next suggestion duplicates a surviving roll's id. Example: create 260611-1 and 260611-2, delete 260611-1, suggest -> count=1 -> '260611-2', which already exists. rolls.roll_id is UNIQUE (migration/src/m20260222_000019_schema_hardening.rs:121), so accepting the prefilled suggestion fails roll creation with a 422 constraint error — confusing when the user did nothing wrong, and it happens exactly in the 'misloaded a roll, delete and re-create' flow.

Why it matters for first release: roll creation is the entry point of the whole lifecycle; a self-inflicted unique-constraint error on the suggested default is a papercut the single user will hit.

Suggested fix: derive the suffix from the existing ids instead of the count, e.g. SELECT roll_id FROM rolls WHERE roll_id LIKE 'YYMMDD-%', parse the numeric tail after the last '-', take max+1 (or do it in SQL with CAST(substr(...) AS INTEGER) — raw SQL is already the pattern here for ORDER BY CAST). Add a unit/integration test: create two, delete the first, suggestion does not collide.
notes: verifier: confirmed — suggest_id (roll_service.rs:356-376) is COUNT(*)+1 over LIKE 'YYMMDD-%'; rolls_new.roll_id TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE (migration 19:121); rolls/new/+page.svelte prefills rollId from suggestRollId(), so accepting the default after deleting an earlier same-day roll 422s. One nuance: the single-roll misload flow (create one, delete, recreate) does NOT collide — collision needs 2+ same-day rolls with a non-last one deleted, so P3 (papercut, not pre-release blocker) is correctly calibrated.

title: Film stocks page has no edit UI (and B&W-slide stocks unreachable via type tabs)
description:
Film stocks can only be added or deleted in the UI — there is no edit dialog — even though the full update path already exists end-to-end.

Evidence:
- frontend/src/routes/(app)/film-stocks/+page.svelte:16 imports only listFilmStocks, createFilmStock, deleteFilmStock; the row at :243-260 renders only a delete button (every comparable entity — lenses, labs, cameras — has Edit).
- frontend/src/lib/api/film-stocks.ts:8-9 — updateFilmStock(id, Partial<FilmStockInsert>) already exists, wired to PUT /api/film-stocks/{id} (src/routes/film_stocks.rs:50 has .put(update)).

Why it matters for first release: a typo'd ISO, exposure count, or name on a stock that rolls already reference cannot be fixed — delete-and-recreate is the only path, and deleting a referenced stock either fails on the FK or orphans the rolls' film_stock_id. Exposure_count also drives the new-roll frame-count auto-fill, so a wrong value keeps propagating.

Minor included fix: the type filter tabs at :212-216 offer All / Color / B&W / Slide where Slide maps only to 'color-slide' — 'bw-slide' stocks are reachable only under All. Add a tab or make Slide match both slide types.

Suggested fix: clone the lenses-page edit-dialog pattern (shared form fields + startEdit/handleEdit using updateFilmStock), reusing the existing add-dialog markup.
notes: verifier: confirmed — film-stocks/+page.svelte:16 imports only list/create/delete (+listDistinctFilmBrands); the row renders a single delete button, no Edit. updateFilmStock exists (api/film-stocks.ts:8-9) and the backend PUT is wired (src/routes/film_stocks.rs:50 .put(update)). Type tabs (:213-216) compare filterType === 'color-slide' for the Slide tab while FilmStockType includes 'bw-slide' (types/index.ts:99), so bw-slide stocks match no tab but All. P3 appropriate — feature gap with a workaround (delete/recreate before referencing, or sqlite3).

title: Developments page omits lab developments — permanently empty for lab-path users
description: The sidebar item 'Developing' (Sidebar.svelte:13) leads to /developments, which calls only listAllSelfDevelopments (frontend/src/routes/(app)/developments/+page.svelte:91) and titles itself 'Developments' with description 'Self-development records across all rolls' (line 104). A user whose rolls all go through labs — a fully supported, arguably primary path — sees a permanently empty page whose EmptyState says 'Self-development records will appear here once you add them to your rolls' (lines 124-130), implying they have no development records when they do. Lab developments (lab, dates, cost) have no list view anywhere; there is no way to answer 'what did I spend at labs this year' or 'which rolls are at which lab' outside per-roll pages. Why it matters: a whole nav destination is dead for half the lifecycle, and the lab/self asymmetry is invisible until discovered by confusion. Suggested fix for first release: either include lab dev records in the page (grouped/tabbed Lab vs Self, lab rows showing lab name, dropped-off/received, cost) or rename the nav item and page to 'Self Dev' so the scope is honest.
notes: verifier: confirmed — developments/+page.svelte:91 fetches only listAllSelfDevelopments, page header (line 104) and EmptyState (lines 124-130) describe self-dev only, and the backend has no list-all lab-dev endpoint at all (development.rs router: lab has only create + for-roll get; only /self has a list route). The 'Developing' nav item (Sidebar.svelte:13) is genuinely dead for a lab-only catalog. P3 calibrated.

title: Labs Add dialog: Cancel/backdrop close skips resetForm, and EmptyState CTA reuses stale form data
description:
CLAUDE.md documents the convention 'Dialog Cancel buttons must call resetForm() to clear stale form data (same as the success path)', but the Labs add dialog violates it:

- frontend/src/routes/(app)/labs/+page.svelte:161 — Cancel is 'onclick={() => (showAddDialog = false)}' with no resetForm().
- labs/+page.svelte:151 — the Dialog has 'bind:open' but no onclose, so closing via the × button or backdrop click also leaves the form populated.
- labs/+page.svelte:121 — the EmptyState '+ Add Lab' button opens the dialog WITHOUT resetForm() (the PageHeader button at line 108 does reset), so a previously cancelled half-typed lab reappears.
- The page also shares one 'error' $state between the page-level load banner (line 112) and both dialogs (lines 157, 174), so a stale load/delete error renders inside a freshly opened Add dialog.

Why it matters for first release: stale form data leads to accidentally saving a half-edited previous entry; it is exactly the failure mode the documented convention exists to prevent, and this page silently drifted from it.

Suggested fix: add 'onclose={resetForm}' to the Add Dialog, call resetForm() in the Cancel handler and in the EmptyState CTA (or simply always reset on open), and clear/scope 'error' when opening a dialog.
notes: verifier: confirmed — labs/+page.svelte:161 Cancel skips resetForm (documented convention), :151 Add Dialog lacks onclose, :121 EmptyState CTA opens without reset, shared error state at :112/:157/:174 leaks page errors into dialogs. Mitigation note: the PageHeader open path (:108) resets on open, so the stale-data repro is mainly via the EmptyState CTA or x/backdrop close — narrow but real. P3 calibrated.

title: Several add/edit dialogs skip resetForm on cancel or X/Esc/backdrop close (stale form data)
description:
CLAUDE.md convention: 'Dialog Cancel buttons must call resetForm() to clear stale form data (same as the success path).' Several dialogs violate it, and the Dialog component's three other close paths (X button, Escape, backdrop click — Dialog.svelte:11-22) bypass reset entirely on every dialog opened via bind:open without an onclose prop.

Cancel buttons that skip reset:
