- frontend/src/routes/(app)/lenses/+page.svelte:388 — Add Lens Cancel only sets showAddDialog=false (resetForm exists and is used by the edit dialog at :431).
- frontend/src/routes/(app)/labs/+page.svelte:161 — Add Lab Cancel.
- frontend/src/routes/(app)/cameras/[id]/+page.svelte:581 — Add Maintenance Cancel never clears maintType/maintDoneBy/maintDateDone/maintCost/maintNotes (:67-71); success path resets inline at :249-253.

bind:open dialogs with no onclose (X/Esc/backdrop leak stale data):
- cameras/+page.svelte:326 (Add Camera), lenses/+page.svelte:359 (Add Lens), film-stocks/+page.svelte:268 (Add Film Stock), labs/+page.svelte:151 (Add Lab), cameras/[id]/+page.svelte:571 (Maintenance) and :598 (Link Lens).

Repro: open Add Camera, type values, press Escape, click '+ Add Camera' again (the open handler at cameras/+page.svelte:233 does not reset) — previous values reappear; half-typed dates can also re-trip the addDateError gate confusingly.

Suggested fix: pass onclose={() => resetForm()} (plus the per-dialog error-string clear) to every form Dialog, and add resetForm to the listed Cancel buttons. Alternatively reset in the open handlers, as lenses' and labs' header buttons already do (:256 / :108) — but their EmptyState '+ Add' buttons (:299 / :121) don't, so onclose is the more robust single point.
notes:
verifier: confirmed — Cancel skips reset at lenses/+page.svelte:388, labs/+page.svelte:161, cameras/[id]/+page.svelte:581 (success path resets at :249-253); all six bind:open dialogs cited (cameras:326, lenses:359, film-stocks:268, labs:151, cameras/[id]:571 & :598) pass no onclose, and Dialog.svelte handleClose (:11-14) only invokes onclose when provided, so X/Esc/backdrop leak stale state. Nuance: cameras (:366) and film-stocks (:287) Cancel buttons DO reset — for those two pages only the X/Esc/backdrop paths leak (open handlers :233/:276 and :189/:231 do not reset, so the Escape-then-reopen repro holds). P3 appropriate (convention/polish, no data corruption).
merged kammerz-aun (usability finder): Labs Add dialog cancel/backdrop skips resetForm and the EmptyState CTA reopens the dialog with stale form data — include in the same sweep

title: Status chevron bar affordance and status semantics are unexplained in-app
description: The chevron bar (rolls/[id]/+page.svelte:779-842) is the lifecycle's main control but reads as a static progress indicator: the buttons have no title/tooltip/aria-label, and nothing anywhere in the app explains that (a) clicking a future chevron advances instantly, (b) clicking a past chevron asks for confirmation, (c) clicking the CURRENT at-lab/developing chevron re-opens the dev dialog (the only orphan-recovery affordance, handleStatusClick:554-562), or (d) what the statuses themselves mean (e.g. Shot vs Developed vs Scanned, or that Lab Done differs from Developed). The Development section's empty state just says 'No development info yet.' (DevelopmentSection.svelte:346) without pointing at the Develop chevron — the only way to create a dev record in the whole UI. Why it matters: fine for the author today, but the bar's click-behaviors are rich and invisible; after time away (see kammerz-03p) even the author will forget that clicking the current chevron reopens the dialog. Suggested fix: add title attributes per chevron ('Move to At Lab', 'Move back to Shot (asks to confirm)'), change the Development empty state to 'No development info yet — use the Develop step in the status bar above', and consider a small (?) popover on the Status header summarizing the flows.
notes: verifier: confirmed — chevron buttons (rolls/[id]/+page.svelte:788-800) carry only the status label, no title/aria hints about advance-instant/back-confirm/current-click-reopens-dialog (handleStatusClick:546-562 makes a same-status click with no dev record open the dialog — the only orphan recovery); DevelopmentSection has NO '+ Lab'/'+ Self' buttons anywhere (header lines 261-264 is bare), so the chevron/Develop menu really is the sole dev-record creation path and the empty state 'No development info yet.' (line 346) never points there. P3 calibrated — single-user app, but kammerz-03p (time-away QA) makes the forgetting scenario concrete.

title: Extractor rejections (bad JSON, invalid enum, non-numeric path id) bypass the {error} envelope
description:
src/error.rs defines the documented error contract — every error serializes as {"error":{"code","message"}} (CLAUDE.md: 'errors serialize as {error:{code,message}}'; src/main.rs:118-121 even special-cases unmatched /api/* 404s to keep the envelope byte-identical). But axum's built-in extractor rejections never reach AppError:

- Json<T> body failures — malformed JSON (400), wrong types or an invalid enum string like status: "bogus" (422), missing Content-Type (415) — return axum's plain-text rejection body.
- Path<i32> with a non-numeric id (e.g. GET /api/rolls/abc) returns a plain-text 400.
- Query<SearchQuery> with a missing ?q (src/routes/search.rs:20-24) returns a plain-text 400.

frontend client.ts:40-51 has a catch-all that falls back to res.statusText, so the user sees an opaque 'Unprocessable Entity' instead of axum's actually-useful rejection text ('Failed to deserialize the JSON body at line ...'), and error.code is 'UNKNOWN'.

Why it matters for first release: invalid enum strings are the most likely drift bug (CLAUDE.md notes Rust enums and the TS unions must be updated in tandem); when they drift, every affected save fails with a blank-looking error that is painful to diagnose from a phone.

Suggested fix: use axum::extract::rejection types with WithRejection (axum-extra) or small wrapper extractors (AppJson<T>, AppPath<T>) that convert the rejection into AppError::UnprocessableEntity(rejection.body_text()), preserving the envelope and the useful message.
notes: verifier: confirmed — grep finds no WithRejection/wrapper extractors (only RequireAuth implements FromRequestParts), so axum's plain-text Json/Path/Query rejection bodies bypass the envelope that main.rs:115-121 deliberately preserves even for unmatched /api/* 404s; client.ts:40-51 then discards the useful text and shows statusText with code UNKNOWN. P3 calibrated — diagnosability, not data integrity.

title: No UI to create lens mounts — createLensMount API exists but is unreachable from the frontend
description:
frontend/src/lib/api/lens-mounts.ts:6 defines createLensMount (POST /api/lens-mounts), but no component ever imports it — every usage of the module across frontend/src is listLensMounts only (cameras/+page.svelte:18, cameras/[id]/+page.svelte:16, rolls/new/+page.svelte:16, rolls/[id]/+page.svelte:29, quick-entry/+page.svelte:14, lenses/+page.svelte:18), and there is no lens-mounts route in frontend/src/routes/(app)/. Mounts only exist via seed migrations (migration/src/m20250219_000006_lens_mount_table.rs and follow-ups).

Why it matters for first release: cameras and lenses both require picking a mount; the moment the user acquires gear with a mount not in the seed list (e.g. a new RF or M42 variant not seeded), the only path is curl against the API or sqlite3 on the NAS — a dead end inside an otherwise complete CRUD UI. Camera/lens create dialogs would silently force a wrong mount choice.

Suggested fix: smallest viable — an inline '+ New mount…' option in the mount Select of the camera and lens create/edit dialogs that prompts for a name and calls createLensMount, then refreshes the list (the inline-creation pattern already exists for fixed-lens camera creation). A full lens-mounts management page is not needed for a single-user catalog.
notes: verifier: confirmed — backend POST exists (src/routes/lens_mounts.rs:22 .post(create)), frontend createLensMount defined (api/lens-mounts.ts:6), grep shows zero importers (all 6 consumers import listLensMounts only) and no (app)/lens-mounts route. P3 calibrated.

title: Creating a dev record on a roll orphaned at a sibling-path status no-ops, leaving status contradicting the dev record
description:
advance_status_along (src/services/roll_service.rs:251-276) deliberately no-ops when the roll's current status is not in the target flow — the docstring says this protects 'the sibling dev path'. That rationale assumes a sibling dev RECORD exists, but the same no-op fires for a no-record orphan: a roll sitting at 'developing'/'developed' with NO self dev (importable — the import page offers all 10 statuses with no dev record, frontend/src/routes/(app)/import/+page.svelte:95-106; also reachable via PUT /api/rolls/{id}) where the user then records a LAB dev (the film actually went to a lab). sync_lab_dev_status (roll_service.rs:282-293) finds 'developing' off LAB_FLOW and returns Ok(false), so the roll keeps a self-path status while its only dev record is a lab one. Symmetric for at-lab/lab-done + create_self_dev.

Resulting state: getDevPath returns 'lab' (record priority), the chevron bar uses labFlow, statusFlow.indexOf('developing') === -1 → currentStatusIdx -1, all chevrons render as future and any click is an unconfirmed forward write (frontend/src/routes/(app)/rolls/[id]/+page.svelte:112,546). Recoverable with one manual click, but the auto-sync that owns this logic silently left a status that contradicts the data.

Related: kammerz-afc covers the SAME-flow orphan (e.g. orphaned at-lab + lab dev with date_received → lab-done), which advance_status_along already handles. This is the cross-flow variant, explicitly outside kammerz-afc's acceptance criteria.

Suggested fix: in sync_lab_dev_status / sync_self_dev_status, when the current status is on the SIBLING flow's exclusive segment (at-lab/lab-done vs developing/developed) AND no sibling dev record exists (cheap count inside the same transaction — and if kammerz-ysw lands, the sibling record can't exist at create time at all), treat it as 'shot-equivalent' and set the data-driven target on the new path. Add a backend test: roll at 'developing' (no self dev) + create lab dev with date_received → 'lab-done'.
notes: verifier: confirmed — LAB_FLOW (roll_service.rs:85-94) excludes developing/developed, so sync_lab_dev_status no-ops via advance_status_along's (None, _) arm for a roll imported at 'developing' (import/+page.svelte statusOptions offers all 10 statuses, no dev record created); symmetric for self-dev on at-lab/lab-done. Distinct from kammerz-afc (same-flow orphan, already handled by advance_status_along). P3 calibrated: import-only edge case, recoverable with one manual chevron click, no data loss.

title: ComboInput label is not associated with its input and has no combobox ARIA semantics
description:
frontend/src/lib/components/ui/ComboInput.svelte:74-88 renders the label as a bare '<span>' inside a '<div>' next to the '<input>' — unlike Input.svelte:13, Select.svelte:13, and DateInput.svelte:36, which all wrap the control in a '<label>' element. Consequences: screen readers announce an unlabeled textbox for Brand / Purchased From / Done By fields (cameras/+page.svelte:329,358; cameras/[id]/+page.svelte:346,367,574; film-stocks/+page.svelte:271; lenses/+page.svelte usage), and clicking the label text doesn't focus the input (a real paper-cut on phone where tap targets are small).

The autocomplete dropdown also exposes no ARIA: no role='combobox'/aria-expanded/aria-autocomplete on the input, no role='listbox'/'option' on the popup (ComboInput.svelte:89-101), and no aria-activedescendant for the keyboard highlight — so the (otherwise functional) ArrowUp/Down/Enter keyboard support at lines 41-71 is invisible to assistive tech.

Why it matters for first release: ComboInput is used on every gear-entry form; matching the label wiring of the sibling components is cheap and removes the worst of it.

Suggested fix: wrap in '<label>' like Input.svelte does (the dropdown div can stay inside; option buttons use onmousedown so label-induced blur ordering is unaffected — verify the blur timeout still works), then add role='combobox', aria-expanded={showDropdown}, role='listbox'/role='option' + aria-selected, and aria-activedescendant for the highlight.
notes: verifier: confirmed — ComboInput.svelte:74-77 bare span (vs <label> wrap in Input.svelte:13/Select.svelte:13/DateInput.svelte:36); input at 78-88 and dropdown at 89-101 carry no combobox/listbox/option/aria-activedescendant despite keyboard nav at 41-71. P3 calibrated.

title: Import LLM endpoints have no HTTP timeout; duplicate import frame numbers fail with a misleading message
description:
Two robustness gaps in the AI note-import path:

1. No timeout on outbound Anthropic calls. ImportService::list_models and ImportService::parse_note (src/services/import_service.rs:125 and 175) build reqwest::Client::new() with default settings — reqwest has NO total request timeout by default. A stalled upstream connection leaves GET /api/import/models or POST /api/import/parse hanging indefinitely; from the field over VPN on a phone the user just sees a spinner forever, and the server keeps the request alive with no way to cancel. Suggested fix: one shared lazy Client built with .timeout(Duration::from_secs(60)) (parse with a long max_tokens response can be slow, but should still be bounded) and .connect_timeout(Duration::from_secs(10)).

2. Misleading error for duplicate frame numbers. The LLM prompt encourages 'Same'-propagation and range notation, so duplicate frame_number values in the parsed payload are plausible. RollService::import_roll inserts shots under the UNIQUE(roll_id, frame_number) index (initial schema m20250101_000001 lines 239-248); the transaction correctly rolls back, but the handler maps the error with friendly_err("roll", e) (src/routes/import.rs:162-164), producing 'A roll with that frame number already exists.' — wrong noun, and it doesn't say which frame collided. Suggested fix: pre-validate shot entries for duplicate/empty frame_number in import_parsed_roll and return a targeted 422 ('shots[3]: duplicate frame number "12"') before opening the transaction.

The transactionality itself is correct (roll + shots + lenses all-or-nothing, src/services/roll_service.rs:171-215).
notes: verifier: confirmed — reqwest::Client::new() at import_service.rs:125 and 175 (reqwest has no default total timeout, so a stalled upstream hangs /api/import/parse and /models indefinitely); import.rs:164 maps the UNIQUE(roll_id,frame_number) rollback via friendly_err("roll", e) → 'A roll with that frame number already exists.' (wrong noun, no colliding frame identified). P3 calibrated for an optional AI-assist path.

title: Confirmed backward status moves are silently undone by unrelated dev-record edits
description: Verified sequence: roll at lab-done with date_received set; user clicks a backward chevron to at-lab and confirms via ConfirmDialog ('the lab is redoing it') — updateStatus writes status only, never touching dates (rolls/[id]/+page.svelte:480-515). Later the user edits ANY field of the lab dev record (even notes): handleSaveLabDev sends the full payload including the still-set date_received (DevelopmentSection.svelte:159-182), and the update route calls resync_lab_dev_status with has_received=true (src/routes/development.rs:234), which advance_status_along moves at-lab back up to lab-done (src/services/roll_service.rs:317-329). The deliberate, explicitly-confirmed backward move is silently reverted by an edit that had nothing to do with status. Same applies to developed->developing via resync_self_dev_status (development.rs:423). CLAUDE.md says the backward-confirm exists precisely so the user can 'intentionally contradict the data state' — but the contradiction survives only until the next dev-record edit. Why it matters: combined with the no-feedback gap (kammerz-9xg) the user's correction just vanishes. Suggested fix: in the lab/self dev update handlers, only call resync when the status-driving date field actually changed in this update (compare old vs new date_received / date_processed before resyncing); alternatively have the UI hint that clearing the date is the supported way to move back.
notes: verifier: confirmed — handleSaveLabDev always re-sends date_received (DevelopmentSection.svelte:162-172), update_lab_dev unconditionally calls resync_lab_dev_status(result.date_received.is_some()) (development.rs:234-239), and advance_status_along moves at-lab back up to lab-done since cur<tgt on LAB_FLOW. A confirmed backward move with the date still set is silently re-advanced by any dev edit; mirror path for self via development.rs:423. Real design tension (data-driven sync vs CLAUDE.md's 'intentionally contradict the data state'); P3 calibrated — edge sequence, and clearing the date is the data-consistent workaround.

title: suggest_id re-suggests an existing roll_id after a same-day deletion (COUNT-based)
description:
RollService::suggest_id (src/services/roll_service.rs:356-376) suggests '{YYMMDD}-{COUNT(LIKE prefix)+1}'. Counting rows instead of taking the max suffix means deletions create collisions:

1. Create 250611-1 and 250611-2 today.
2. Delete 250611-1.
3. GET /api/rolls/suggest-id -> COUNT=1 -> suggests '250611-2', which still exists.
4. Accepting the suggestion fails on the UNIQUE roll_id constraint (rolls.roll_id UNIQUE, migration m20260222_000019 line 121) with 'A roll with that roll id already exists.'

Also counts any roll_id merely *starting* with today's prefix (LIKE '250611-%' matches a hand-entered '250611-experiments'), inflating the suffix.

Why it matters: the suggestion exists precisely for quick field entry on a phone; a wrong suggestion turns a one-tap flow into manual editing after a confusing duplicate error.

Suggested fix: SELECT MAX(CAST(SUBSTR(roll_id, LENGTH(prefix)+2) AS INTEGER)) FROM rolls WHERE roll_id LIKE '{prefix}-%' and suggest max+1 (CAST of non-numeric tails is 0, so stray ids are harmless).
close_reason: verifier: duplicate of kammerz-cg1 — identical finding (COUNT-based suggest_id at roll_service.rs:356-376 collides after same-day deletion, same MAX-suffix fix). Claim itself is valid and remains tracked in kammerz-cg1.

title: Decide and enforce lab/self dev mutual exclusivity in the backend
description: CLAUDE.md states 'Lab and self dev are mutually exclusive' but only the frontend enforces it (it hides the + Lab / + Self buttons once one record exists). The backend happily creates both: create_lab_dev (src/routes/development.rs:147-187) and create_self_dev (development.rs:304-358) never check the sibling table, and migration m20260222_000019_schema_hardening.rs only adds per-table UNIQUE(roll_id) indexes (lines 25-26) — one row in EACH table for the same roll is allowed. Consequences when both exist: getDevPath prioritizes lab unconditionally (frontend/src/lib/utils/status.ts:162), the sibling-guard in the delete handlers (development.rs:269-283, 458-472) suppresses status reverts, and a create on the second path silently no-ops its status sync because the roll sits on the other flow (advance_status_along returns false for off-flow statuses, src/services/roll_service.rs:262-275). Why it matters for first release: this is a documented invariant enforced only client-side; the AI-import path or any direct API call can violate it, and breaking-change fixes are free now. Suggested fix: inside the create transactions, count the sibling table for roll_id and return 422 (a roll already has a lab/self development) — mirroring the existing has_self_dev/has_lab_dev queries in the delete handlers — plus a small integration test for each direction. Alternatively, decide both-allowed is intended (the delete-handler guards suggest tolerance) and update CLAUDE.md + add a test pinning the both-records behavior.
close_reason: verifier: duplicate of kammerz-ysw [P1 bug], which covers the same backend-unenforced lab/self mutual-exclusion hole (development.rs:147-187/:304-358, migration 019 per-table UNIQUE only) with a fuller consequence analysis (stranded statuses, chevron rendering) and the same suggested fix. Finding itself is factually correct.

title: No feedback when roll status auto-changes — user cannot tell why a status moved
description: Backend auto-sync rewrites roll status on many mutations: shot create/delete (src/routes/shots.rs:131,238), dev create/update/delete (src/routes/development.rs:177,234,276,344,423,465), and Timeline date edits route through the same updateLabDev/updateSelfDev (rolls/[id]/+page.svelte:521-529), so editing 'Received back' in the Timeline can silently flip at-lab to lab-done. The frontend just calls loadRollData() afterwards (rolls/[id]/+page.svelte:148-149 comment) and the chevron/Badge repaint with no announcement; the app has no toast/notification primitive at all. A user who edits a dev record or adds a date and later notices the status moved has no way to know what moved it or when. Why it matters: the data-driven sync is the system's cleverest behavior, and invisible cleverness reads as bugs — especially during the first weeks of real use. Suggested fix: cheap frontend-only version — in loadRollData, compare previous roll.status to the freshly fetched one and, when they differ and the change was not an explicit chevron click, show a small transient notice near the Status section ('Status advanced to At Lab because a lab development was recorded'). A minimal Toast/Inline-notice component would also serve the dev-dialog flows in kammerz-zoo.
notes: verifier: confirmed — auto-sync rewrites status at all cited call sites (shots.rs:131,238; development.rs:177,234,276,344,423,465), frontend just re-fetches via loadRollData with no diffing, and repo-wide grep finds no toast/snackbar/notice primitive anywhere in frontend/src. Silent repaint is real; P3 calibrated (feedback polish, not a data hazard — the Timeline still shows the driving dates).

title: DELETE of a missing id returns 204 on non-transactional routes but 404 on transactional ones
description:
PR #31 (kammerz-rwa) established that deleting a missing id should be 404: the transactional delete handlers (DELETE /api/shots/{id} src/routes/shots.rs:214-254, /api/development/lab/{id} development.rs:250-292, /api/development/self/{id} development.rs:439-481) look the row up with or_404_db and return 404 for a stale id.

The non-transactional deletes never check existence: RollService::delete (src/services/roll_service.rs:149-152), CameraService::delete (camera_service.rs:30-33), delete_maintenance (camera_service.rs:62-65), LensService::delete (lens_service.rs:29-32), FilmStockService::delete, LabService::delete all call delete_by_id().exec() and ignore rows_affected, so DELETE /api/rolls/999999 returns 204 NO_CONTENT.

So the API answers the same question two different ways: DELETE /api/shots/{missing} -> 404, DELETE /api/rolls/{missing} -> 204. (Related context, deliberate per CLAUDE.md RPC-parity: GET /api/rolls/{missing} returns 200 with null body while GET /api/rolls/{missing}/detail returns 404 — not proposing to change the GETs, just noting the delete inconsistency is the part that contradicts the kammerz-rwa decision.)

Why it matters for first release: the frontend treats 204 as success, so a double-tap/stale-tab delete silently 'succeeds' on these routes instead of surfacing 'already deleted'; and fixing it later is a behavior change for API consumers.

Suggested fix: check DeleteResult::rows_affected == 0 (or do an or_404 lookup first) in the six non-transactional delete services/handlers and return AppError::NotFound, matching the transactional handlers.
notes: verifier: confirmed — RollService::delete (roll_service.rs:149-152) discards DeleteResult; grep finds no rows_affected checks in camera/lens/film_stock/lab services or delete_maintenance, while PR #31 (commit 8575b67) made transactional deletes 404 on missing ids. Real inconsistency vs the kammerz-rwa decision. P3 calibrated.

title: Update or delete stale Tauri-era TEST_PLAN.md
description: TEST_PLAN.md is a pre-port artifact: line 5 says run with 'bun run tauri dev' and line 6 gives the DB location as ~/Library/Application Support/com.kammerz.app/kammerz.db — neither exists anymore (the app is axum+web, started with 'just dev', dev DB at ./kammerz.db per DATABASE_URL). Its section 1.2 also lists the In the Darkroom statuses without post-processed (same drift as CLAUDE.md). Why it matters for first release: existing bead kammerz-03p plans a manual QA smoke pass, and this stale plan is exactly the document that pass would reach for — following it fails at step one and its checklists silently omit newer behavior (path-aware chevrons, post-processed, login/auth flows, rate limiting). Suggested fix: either refresh the header/run instructions and sweep the checklists against current routes, or delete it and fold manual-QA steps into kammerz-03p; do not leave instructions for a runtime that no longer exists in the repo.
notes: verifier: confirmed — TEST_PLAN.md:5-6 still says 'bun run tauri dev' with DB at ~/Library/Application Support/com.kammerz.app/ (line 390 repeats the tauri command), and section 1.2 line 22 lists Darkroom statuses without post-processed. Not a duplicate of kammerz-03p (that bead is the QA pass itself; this is fixing/deleting the doc it would use). P3 calibrated.

title: Fix CLAUDE.md status-flow docs: post-processed status is missing everywhere
description: CLAUDE.md never mentions the post-processed roll status, but it is a full status in code: entity/src/roll.rs:31 (string_value post-processed), frontend/src/lib/utils/status.ts:69-74 and 88-137 (statusConfig + every flow array place it between scanned and archived), src/services/roll_service.rs:92,104 (LAB_FLOW/SELF_FLOW), migration m20260602_000021_add_roll_lifecycle_dates.rs (rolls.date_post_processed column). Stale CLAUDE.md claims: (1) Roll status progression bullet lists labFlow as loaded->shooting->shot->at-lab->lab-done->scanned->archived (and selfFlow/undecidedFlow likewise) — all omit post-processed; (2) Dashboard roll sections bullet says In the Darkroom shows shot/at-lab/lab-done/developing/developed/scanned, but the code includes post-processed (frontend/src/routes/(app)/+page.svelte:40 processingStatuses). Why it matters for first release: CLAUDE.md is the instruction set for agents working on this repo — anyone building a new status-aware feature from the doc will re-omit the status and break the 'all non-archived rolls appear in at least one dashboard section' invariant. Suggested fix: update the two bullets (and any other flow enumerations) to include post-processed, and mention the date_post_processed lifecycle column.
notes: verifier: confirmed — grep finds zero 'post-processed' mentions in CLAUDE.md while the status exists in entity/src/roll.rs:31-33, status.ts flows (:95,:107,:117,:135), roll_service.rs LAB_FLOW/SELF_FLOW (:92,:104), dashboard processingStatuses (+page.svelte:40), and migration 021's date_post_processed column. CLAUDE.md's labFlow/selfFlow/undecidedFlow and 'In the Darkroom' enumerations all end scanned->archived. P3 calibrated (doc-only fix, but CLAUDE.md drift actively misleads agents).

title: Server-side validation gaps: empty required strings, negative numbers, unbounded GPS coordinates
description:
The backend's own stance (src/validate.rs:1-10, kammerz-igc) is that invalid data must never persist regardless of client, but date fields are the only ones validated server-side. Verified gaps:

Empty required strings (trim collapses to '' which satisfies NOT NULL):
- POST /api/cameras with brand/model of whitespace -> camera named ' ' persists (src/routes/cameras.rs:144-145, trim() at src/patch.rs:28-30 happily stores empty string).
- POST /api/rolls with roll_id '' persists (src/routes/rolls.rs:124); UNIQUE means only the first succeeds, later ones get a confusing duplicate error.
- POST /api/labs name '' (src/routes/labs.rs:70), POST /api/film-stocks brand/name '' (src/routes/film_stocks.rs:77-78).
- POST /api/lens-mounts doesn't even trim: src/routes/lens_mounts.rs:39 uses Set(data.name) raw, inconsistent with every other handler, so '  Fixed Lens ' creates a mount that defeats the name-based fixed-lens detection convention.

Numbers with no range checks:
- cost (maintenance src/routes/cameras.rs:396, lab dev src/routes/development.rs:165) accepts negative values, which then skew /api/stats totals (stats_service.rs:88-106).
- iso/exposure_count (film_stocks.rs:80-82), frame_count (rolls.rs:129), duration_seconds/sort_order (development.rs:99-104), filter_thread_*_mm (lenses.rs:113-114) all accept negative/absurd values.
- gps_lat/gps_lon (shots.rs:33-34) accept any f64; should be -90..=90 / -180..=180.

Why it matters: the frontend validates, but the API is the durable contract for a network-reachable app; bad rows are annoying to clean up in SQLite by hand and some (negative cost) silently corrupt stats.

Suggested fix: a small validate.rs companion — require_nonempty(field, &str), validate_range helpers — applied in create/update handlers alongside validate_date_opt; trim the lens_mount name.
notes: verifier: confirmed — grep shows validate_date_opt is the only server-side validation in src/routes/; patch.rs:28-30 trim() persists '' for required strings; lens_mounts.rs:39 is raw Set(data.name) with no trim (breaks name-based Fixed Lens detection for padded names); shots.rs gps_lat/gps_lon are unbounded f64. Not a dup of kammerz-b26 (that one is client-side edit forms). P3 calibrated — frontend gates required fields, single trusted user.

title: Add a cross-check for status flows duplicated between status.ts and roll_service.rs
description: The roll status flows are hand-duplicated across four places with no cross-check: LAB_FLOW/SELF_FLOW in src/services/roll_service.rs:85-106, labFlow/selfFlow/undecidedFlow + allStatusOrder in frontend/src/lib/utils/status.ts:88-137, the Rust RollStatus enum in entity/src/roll.rs:6-35, and the TypeScript RollStatus union in frontend/src/lib/types/index.ts. CLAUDE.md itself acknowledges the manual sync rule (when adding enum variants, update both) but nothing fails if one side is missed — the post-processed status was evidently added late and CLAUDE.md was never updated to include it (separate bead), demonstrating the drift mode. Why it matters: a frontend chevron bar showing a status the backend advance_status_along does not know about (or vice versa) produces silent no-op syncs or unreachable statuses. Suggested fix (pick one, cheap pre-release): (a) commit a canonical JSON fixture (e.g. shared/status-flows.json) asserted by a Rust unit test and imported by status.ts so TypeScript type-checks it against the RollStatus union; or (b) expose GET /api/meta/status-flows from the backend constants and assert equality with the frontend arrays in the Playwright smoke suite (frontend/tests/smoke.spec.ts) which already runs against the real binary in CI.
notes: verifier: confirmed — four uncross-checked hand copies verified in sync today (roll_service.rs:85-106 LAB_FLOW/SELF_FLOW, status.ts:88-137 flows+allStatusOrder, entity/src/roll.rs:6-37 enum incl. PostProcessed, types/index.ts:132-141 union incl. post-processed); nothing in CI fails on drift, and the doc-side copy (CLAUDE.md) already drifted by omitting post-processed (kammerz-njk), demonstrating the mode. P3 calibrated — code copies match today, so this is preventive.

title: friendly_err maps FK failures to a delete-specific message on create/update paths
description:
src/routes/mod.rs:93-98: friendly_err unconditionally maps 'FOREIGN KEY constraint failed' to 'Cannot delete this {context} because it is still referenced by other records.' The comment even notes '(usually on delete)' — but FK violations also fire on create/update with a stale/invalid FK id, and the same closure is used on every create/update handler.

Concrete repro paths:
- POST /api/shots with a roll_id whose roll was just deleted (e.g. second browser tab) -> 422 'Cannot delete this shot because it is still referenced by other records.'
- POST /api/rolls or PUT /api/rolls/{id} with a camera_id/film_stock_id/lens_id pointing at a deleted row -> 'Cannot delete this roll ...'
- PUT /api/maintenance/{id} moving camera_id to a nonexistent camera (src/routes/cameras.rs:423-425) -> same wrong message.

Why it matters for first release: stale-id races are exactly the case where the user needs a clear message; the current one tells them the opposite of what happened and mentions an operation they didn't perform.

Suggested fix: give friendly_err an operation hint (e.g. friendly_err_for(op: Op::Create|Update|Delete, context, e)) or word the FK branch neutrally: 'This {context} references a record that no longer exists, or is still referenced by other records.' Alternatively, validate FK ids exist up front in create/update handlers and return a targeted 422.
notes: verifier: confirmed — mod.rs:94-98 FK branch is unconditionally delete-worded, and the same friendly_err closure wraps create/update paths (rolls.rs:144 create, shots.rs:143/207 create, cameras.rs:444 maintenance update). With foreign_keys=ON at runtime, a stale FK id on POST yields the 'Cannot delete' message. P3 calibrated: misleading text in a rare stale-id race, not a data bug.

title: Commit bun.lock so frontend/release builds are reproducible
description: Cargo.lock is committed and CI builds with --locked (.github/workflows/ci.yml:36-37,80), but the frontend deliberately has no lockfile: .gitignore ignores 'bun.lock' and ci.yml:52 notes 'No lockfile is committed... so install fresh'. Every CI run, e2e build, and 'just build' resolves frontend deps from scratch, so (1) any transitive npm release can break CI or — worse — silently change the shipped SPA between two builds of the same commit, and (2) a tagged release binary (kammerz-g18) is not reproducible: rebuilding the same tag later can embed different JS. For a release-ready single binary, both halves should be pinned. Fix: remove 'bun.lock' from .gitignore, commit the lockfile, and use 'bun install --frozen-lockfile' in ci.yml (both frontend and e2e jobs) and the justfile build/check recipes; update the ci.yml comment.
notes: verifier: confirmed — .gitignore:39 ignores bun.lock; frontend/bun.lock exists in the working tree but is untracked (git ls-files: no lockfile of any kind committed); ci.yml comments 'No lockfile is committed... so install fresh' and runs bare 'bun install' in both frontend and e2e jobs while cargo uses --locked. Real reproducibility gap for tagged release binaries (pairs with kammerz-g18). P3 calibrated.

title: Serve ETag/304 for non-immutable embedded assets (fonts, index.html)
description:
src/main.rs:131-141 (serve_spa) sets Cache-Control 'public, max-age=31536000, immutable' for _app/immutable/* (correct) but 'no-cache' for everything else — with NO validator (no ETag, no Last-Modified). 'no-cache' without a validator means the browser cannot send a conditional request, so it re-downloads the full body on every full page load. That hits the four self-hosted fonts referenced from app.css (frontend/static/fonts/: dm-sans 37KB + 2x ibm-plex-mono 10KB + instrument-serif 15KB ≈ 72KB total), plus index.html, favicon, etc. — none of which are content-hashed by the build because static/ files are copied verbatim outside _app/immutable. Over the field VPN on a phone, every cold navigation/reload pays ~72KB+ for bytes that essentially never change.

Why for first release: cheap fix, and the field-over-VPN phone path is the app's stated remote use case; combined with missing compression (kammerz-5n3) it makes full reloads needlessly slow.

Suggested fix: rust-embed exposes a content hash via EmbeddedFile metadata (content.metadata.sha256_hash()). In serve_spa, emit it as a strong ETag header on every asset response, and check the request's If-None-Match first, returning 304 Not Modified with empty body on match (requires taking headers/Request in the handler signature). Keep the immutable Cache-Control for _app/immutable/*; the ETag makes the 'no-cache' bucket (fonts, index.html, robots.txt) revalidate with a ~100-byte 304 instead of a full re-download.
notes: verifier: confirmed — serve_spa (main.rs:131-141) sends Cache-Control: no-cache with no ETag/Last-Modified for everything outside _app/immutable/; no-cache without a validator forces full re-download per cold document load. Measured fonts in frontend/build/fonts: 36980+10052+10060+15040 = 72,132 bytes — exactly the claimed ~72KB; index.html is 1.7KB. rust-embed 8 does expose metadata().sha256_hash() for a strong ETag. Not subsumed by kammerz-5n3: woff2 is pre-compressed so gzip gains ~nothing here, 304s are the only win. P3 stands (impact is modest — once per cold load — but the fix is small and it is the stated field-VPN path). Simpler alternative also acceptable: long max-age on /fonts/* since the self-hosted font files effectively never change.

title: Operability logging gaps: failed logins unlogged, request logs invisible at default level, RUST_LOG/SQLITE_BUSY_TIMEOUT_MS undocumented
description: Three related gaps that make the deployed app hard to diagnose from 'journalctl -u kammerz': (1) Failed login attempts produce no log line — src/auth/handlers.rs login() returns AppError::Unauthorized on a bad password, and error.rs:19-46 only logs the Internal variant. On a VPN-reachable single-password app, the operator can't see brute-force attempts (the rate limiter throttles them but logs nothing either) or distinguish 'user typo' from the kammerz-ao1 mangled-hash lockout. Add a tracing::warn!(ip, "failed login") in the handler. (2) tower_http::TraceLayer (src/main.rs:89, new_for_http defaults) emits spans/on_response at DEBUG and only failures (5xx) at ERROR — with tracing_subscriber::fmt::init()'s INFO default (src/main.rs:49) there are no per-request logs at all, so 404/422 mysteries ('why did my save fail?') leave no server-side trace. Either configure DefaultOnResponse::new().level(Level::INFO) or document RUST_LOG=tower_http=debug as the debugging knob. (3) Env-var documentation drift: RUST_LOG (honored via the env-filter feature, Cargo.toml:35) and SQLITE_BUSY_TIMEOUT_MS (src/db.rs:21) exist but appear in neither .env.example nor README's Configuration section — every supported env var should be discoverable from .env.example. All three are small; bundling because the fix is one pass over logging + docs.
notes: verifier: confirmed all three — (1) login() (handlers.rs:37) returns AppError::Unauthorized and error.rs IntoResponse logs only the Internal variant, so bad passwords (and the kammerz-ao1 lockout) leave zero journalctl trace; (2) tower-http 0.6 DEFAULT_MESSAGE_LEVEL is Level::DEBUG (verified in trace/make_span.rs), invisible at fmt::init()'s INFO default; (3) grep finds RUST_LOG/SQLITE_BUSY_TIMEOUT_MS in neither README nor .env.example despite env-filter being enabled (Cargo.toml:35). ConnectInfo<SocketAddr> is already installed (main.rs:101) so warn!(ip) is straightforward. P3 calibrated.

title: Add HTTP response compression (gzip/br) to the axum server
description:
tower-http is built with only the 'trace' feature (Cargo.toml:27) and main.rs:86-89 layers only TraceLayer — no CompressionLayer anywhere. Every response goes over the wire uncompressed: the ~568KB first-load JS/CSS bundle (frontend/build/_app), and all JSON API payloads. The unbounded list endpoints are the ones that grow: GET /api/rolls (RollService::list_all_with_details, src/services/roll_service.rs:109-119) returns every roll with joined camera/film/lens names and full notes text, and is fetched by the dashboard, the rolls list, and quick entry on every visit. At a realistic 100-200 rolls/year this payload reaches several hundred KB uncompressed within a few years — noticeable on a phone over the field VPN, where this app is explicitly meant to be used. JSON/JS compress 5-10x.

Why for first release: it is a two-line change with outsized effect on the primary remote-use path (cellular over UniFi VPN).

Suggested fix: add 'compression-gzip' (and optionally 'compression-br') to the tower-http features in Cargo.toml, then '.layer(tower_http::compression::CompressionLayer::new())' on the router in src/main.rs (place it so it wraps both the API routes and serve_spa fallback). Verify with: curl -H 'Accept-Encoding: gzip' -sI http://localhost:3002/api/rolls | grep -i content-encoding.
notes: verifier: confirmed — Cargo.toml:27 has tower-http features=["trace"] only; main.rs:86-89 layers just session+TraceLayer (no CompressionLayer anywhere). Measured frontend/build/_app at exactly 568KB, and listRolls (GET /api/rolls, unbounded list_all_with_details at roll_service.rs:109-119) is fetched by dashboard, rolls list, quick-entry, AND camera detail. No duplicate bead exists. P3 calibration is right: payloads are small today at single-user scale, but the fix is ~two lines with 5-10x transfer reduction on the stated phone-over-VPN path. Note: woff2 fonts won't benefit (already compressed) — that's kammerz-jax's territory.

title: Make /api/health verify the database and report the version
description: src/routes/mod.rs:62-64: health() returns a static Json(json!({"ok": true})) — it proves the process accepts TCP, nothing more. A wedged/locked/deleted-out-from-under DB (e.g., the data dir unmounted on a NAS, or a stuck writer holding the single connection — the pool is max=min=1, src/db.rs:44-46) still reports healthy. The endpoint is already used as a readiness probe by CI (.github/workflows/ci.yml:99) and would be the natural target for uptime-kuma/systemd watchdog checks on the NAS. Suggested fix: give health() State<DatabaseConnection> and run a trivial query (db.execute_unprepared("SELECT 1") or ping()); on failure return 503 with the {error:{code,message}} envelope; include "version": env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION") in the success body so a deployed instance is identifiable (pairs with kammerz-g18). Keep it unauthenticated (it leaks nothing beyond liveness + version on a LAN/VPN-only app).
notes: verifier: confirmed — health() in src/routes/mod.rs returns static json!({"ok":true}) with no State<DatabaseConnection>; pool is max=min=1 (db.rs:44-46) so one wedged writer stalls all queries while health stays green; ci.yml:99 already uses the endpoint as a readiness probe. P3 calibrated. Overlaps the version-surfacing item in kammerz-g18 — implement together.

title: Make the bind address configurable (hardcoded 0.0.0.0) — limit accidental exposure, especially in open-auth mode
description: src/main.rs:92 binds unconditionally to 0.0.0.0:{PORT}. Combined with OPEN mode (no KAMMERZ_PASSWORD_HASH → RequireAuth passes everyone, src/auth/middleware.rs:46-48), the only guard against accidental network exposure is a single startup tracing::warn (src/main.rs:55-60) that nobody sees once the binary runs as a NAS service. Concrete scenarios: a NAS with UPnP/port-forwarding or a second bridged interface exposes the whole catalog API (and, until kammerz-64j lands, the Anthropic API key) with zero auth; and the .env.example reverse-proxy deployment would ideally bind loopback-only so the proxy is the sole ingress. Suggested fix: read BIND_ADDR (or HOST) from env, default 0.0.0.0 to preserve current behavior, document 127.0.0.1 in .env.example for proxy deployments. Optional belt-and-suspenders for open mode: surface a small persistent 'open access mode' indicator in the UI (frontend already receives auth_required:false from GET /api/auth/me) so the operator notices the unauthenticated state after startup, not just in a scrolled-away log line.
notes: verifier: confirmed — src/main.rs:91-92 reads only PORT from env and hardcodes 0.0.0.0; the open-mode warning at src/main.rs:55-60 is the sole exposure safeguard. P3 correct: LAN-reachable 0.0.0.0 is the intended default for this app, so this is opt-in hardening (BIND_ADDR + loopback for proxy deployments), not a release gate. The optional UI open-mode indicator is sound — GET /api/auth/me already returns auth_required.

title: Login rate limiter collapses to one bucket behind the documented TLS reverse proxy
description: The brute-force guard (src/routes/mod.rs:29-42 + src/auth/rate_limit.rs) uses tower-governor's default PeerIpKeyExtractor, keyed off ConnectInfo<SocketAddr> (src/main.rs:99-102). Direct LAN/VPN clients get distinct IPs — fine. But .env.example explicitly contemplates running 'behind a TLS reverse proxy' (the SECURE_COOKIES comment), and in that topology every request arrives from the proxy's IP: all clients share one 5-burst/10s bucket. Consequences: (a) an attacker hammering /api/auth/login locks the legitimate user out indefinitely (lockout-DoS, trivially sustainable), and (b) the per-client semantics the limiter was designed around silently vanish. Secondary note: the deliberate no-retain_recent decision (rate_limit.rs:10-14) assumes a bounded LAN/VPN keyspace — that assumption also breaks if the proxy forwards real client IPs from the internet someday. Suggested fix (pick one): document hard in .env.example/README that the proxy must enforce its own rate limit on /api/auth/login and that kammerz must not be internet-exposed via a proxy; or add an opt-in KAMMERZ_TRUST_PROXY=true mode that switches to SmartIpKeyExtractor (X-Forwarded-For) — never trust XFF by default since it is client-spoofable when no proxy exists.
notes: verifier: confirmed — GovernorConfigBuilder in src/routes/mod.rs:29-36 sets no key_extractor, so the default PeerIpKeyExtractor keys on ConnectInfo<SocketAddr> (src/main.rs:99-102); .env.example:6-7 explicitly documents the TLS reverse-proxy topology where all clients share the proxy's peer IP and one 5-burst/10s bucket. P3 correct: the lockout-DoS attacker must already be on the LAN/VPN, so it's degraded semantics in one deployment shape, fixable by docs or opt-in trusted-proxy mode.

title: Make dev-record UPDATE data-driven for status (mirror create_lab_dev/create_self_dev)
description:
Follow-up from /code-review on PR #29 (kammerz-afc). That PR made dev-record CREATION data-driven: create_lab_dev/create_self_dev advance the roll status forward based on whether a completing date (date_received / date_processed) was stored — at-lab vs lab-done, developing vs developed (src/services/roll_service.rs sync_lab_dev_status / sync_self_dev_status). The UPDATE handlers (update_lab_dev / update_self_dev in src/routes/development.rs ~lines 187, 329) do a plain field merge with NO status sync, so the data-driven invariant ('the dev record's date fields are the status signal', now documented in CLAUDE.md) is only half-installed: editing an existing record to ADD a received/processed date does not advance at-lab->lab-done or developing->developed, and removing it does not revert.

Largely MASKED today: the canonical 'received back' UX is the chevron + date-prompt path in frontend rolls/[id]/+page.svelte (updateStatus -> writeDateTarget), which writes the dev date AND advances status together. The gap only surfaces when a user edits the record directly via the 'Edit Lab/Self Development' dialog (DevelopmentSection.svelte) instead of clicking the chevron. Hence P3, not a regression — scoped out of the PR-#29 fix because it changes intended behavior beyond the orphan bug.
design:
Option A (preferred): in update_lab_dev/update_self_dev, after the field merge, call the existing sync_lab_dev_status/sync_self_dev_status with result.date_received.is_some()/result.date_processed.is_some() — same forward-only advance the create path uses. Decide revert-on-date-removal semantics: forward-only advance will NOT pull lab-done back to at-lab when the received date is cleared; that asymmetry may be acceptable (mirrors create being advance-only) or may warrant a small explicit revert. Keep advance_status_along forward-only; do not regress the chevron/date-prompt path which already co-writes date + status.

Option B (rejected): teach the frontend Edit dialog to re-trigger a status transition — duplicates state-machine logic the backend owns (same reasoning that picked the backend fix in kammerz-afc).
acceptance_criteria: Creating a lab dev with no received date lands the roll at at-lab; later editing that same record via PUT to add date_received advances it to lab-done in one save. Symmetric for self dev developing->developed via date_processed. The chevron + date-prompt path and the create-time advance are unchanged. cargo test green; a backend test covers update-adds-completing-date -> advances.
close_reason: Made dev-record UPDATE handlers data-driven for roll status (PR #30, merged). resync_lab_dev_status/resync_self_dev_status advance on date-add and revert one rung on date-clear (symmetric); scanned untouched. 5 tests added.

title: Orphan dev-path recovery takes two clicks: generalize auto_sync_status for rolls already past 'shot'
description:
Surfaced by /code-review on PR #28 (kammerz-a7e). After that PR, clicking a lab/self chevron whose dev record is missing opens the dev dialog instead of advancing — good. But for a roll ALREADY orphaned at at-lab/lab-done/developing/developed (only reachable via import of historical rolls), saving the dev record from that dialog does NOT advance the status to where the user clicked.

Root cause: the backend auto_sync_status (src/services/roll_service.rs) is invoked by create_lab_dev / create_self_dev (src/routes/development.rs ~lines 170, 312) with from_statuses = {Loaded, Shooting, Shot}. A roll already at at-lab/lab-done is not in that set, so creating the record is a no-op for status. The user clicked 'Lab Done', the lab dialog opened, they entered + saved date_received, and the roll stays at 'at-lab' — they must click 'Lab Done' a SECOND time (now labDev exists, date already set → advances directly) to land where they intended.

Not a regression (pre-PR-#28 the click silently advanced with no record AND no date, worse for data integrity) and recoverable in two clicks with no data loss, which is why it was scoped out of kammerz-a7e. Filing as the deeper fix.
design:
Two candidate layers:
(1) Backend (preferred, deepest): when creating a lab/self dev record, reconcile the roll's status forward to the matching dev-path status if the roll is at a PRIOR status on that path — not just from {loaded,shooting,shot}. i.e. creating a lab record for a roll at any pre-at-lab OR orphaned-at-lab status should ensure status >= at-lab. Keep it data-driven; don't regress the normal shot→at-lab/developing transitions or the delete-reverts.
(2) Frontend mitigation (shallower): after the auto-prompted dialog saves on an orphan, re-attempt the originally-clicked status transition (thread the intended target through devAutoPrompt). Rejected as the primary fix during review — it changes the 'dialog save just records, advancing is separate' contract and duplicates state-machine logic the backend owns.

Prefer (1). Verify against the data-driven status-sync rules documented in CLAUDE.md (auto_sync_status owns advance-on-create / revert-on-delete).
acceptance_criteria: An imported roll orphaned at at-lab (no lab dev): clicking 'Lab Done' → dialog → entering date_received + Save lands the roll at lab-done (or at minimum at-lab with the record, with a single subsequent intent honored) in ONE logical action, not two. Symmetric for developing→developed (self). Normal shot→at-lab / shot→developing transitions and the delete-reverts are unchanged. cargo test green; a backend test covers create-on-already-advanced-orphan.

title: CI e2e: capture Playwright traces/artifacts on failure
description:
The e2e CI job (added in PR #25, kammerz-f5s) runs Playwright but produces no debuggable artifact when a smoke test fails on the runner.

Root cause: frontend/playwright.config.ts sets `trace: 'on-first-retry'` while `retries: 0` — so a trace is never recorded (the only attempt is never a retry). The CI workflow also has no `actions/upload-artifact` step, so even the `list` reporter output is all a maintainer gets for a remote failure. (PR #25 added server-side log capture via server.log, which covers backend 500s, but not client-side DOM/network/screenshot context.)

Proposed fix:
- frontend/playwright.config.ts: `trace: 'retain-on-failure'` (and optionally `screenshot: 'only-on-failure'`) so a failed test writes a trace.zip on its single attempt.
- .github/workflows/ci.yml (e2e job): add an `actions/upload-artifact@v4` step with `if: failure()` uploading frontend/test-results (and any playwright-report) so the trace is downloadable from the run.

Out of scope for PR #25 because playwright.config.ts was not in that PR's diff (pre-existing, untouched) — surfaced during the third code-review pass on the CI work.
acceptance_criteria: A failed e2e smoke test on CI produces a downloadable Playwright trace artifact: playwright.config.ts records a trace on failure (retries:0 compatible) and the e2e job uploads test-results on failure via actions/upload-artifact.
notes: Surfaced in the /code-review pass on PR #25 (kammerz-f5s). server.log capture already landed in that PR; this is the client-side complement.
close_reason: Merged in PR #26: trace:retain-on-failure + screenshot:only-on-failure in playwright.config.ts, and actions/upload-artifact@v4 (if: failure()) uploading frontend/test-results in the e2e job.

title: Unify status->date mapping: derive STATUS_DATE_TARGET from timeline milestones
description:
The lifecycle-date feature (kammerz-b08, PR #22) introduced STATUS_DATE_TARGET in rolls/[id]/+page.svelte as a second source of truth for status->{record,field}, parallel to the milestone.target mapping that buildRollTimeline already encodes in timeline.ts. They're keyed differently (RollStatus vs milestone key) so they don't 1:1 align, but they encode the same facts. Adding a new dated status requires updating both, and missing STATUS_DATE_TARGET silently skips the transition date prompt with no type error.

Fix: derive STATUS_DATE_TARGET from the timeline milestones (via a status->milestone-key alias) or co-locate both in timeline.ts so there's one definition. Flagged by multiple reviewers in the max-effort review of PR #22.
notes: Related: kammerz-b08, kammerz-a7e (state-machine review).
close_reason: Unified status→date mapping; merged in PR #27

title: Review roll status state-machine: eliminate paths/states that need date 'skipping'
description:
Raised while designing the lifecycle-date-capture feature (confirm-on-transition + editable timeline). That feature drops the 'Skip' affordance in favor of Cancel/Confirm, but the underlying question remains: are there paths through the roll status state machine where a transition shouldn't require a date at all? The dev-path flows (labFlow, selfFlow, undecidedFlow in status.ts) and the date-per-status mapping may have states that exist only as placeholders or that conflate 'status advanced' with 'a dated event happened'.

Goal: audit the three flows and the status->date mapping; identify any status whose transition has no meaningful date (or whose date is redundant with an adjacent state's), and simplify so no transition needs to be skipped. May overlap with the undecidedFlow placeholder chevrons.

Context: owner couldn't recall a concrete use case for skipping a date prompt and suspected the flows could be designed so skipping is never needed.
notes: Related to the lifecycle-date-capture feature (confirm-on-transition + editable Timeline). status.ts: labFlow/selfFlow/undecidedFlow/getFlowForPath; STATUS_DATE_FIELD map in rolls/[id]/+page.svelte.
close_reason: Hardened the two latent silent-advance edges; merged via PR #28. Audit conclusion recorded in bead comment.

comments.author: Steve Loveless
comments.text:
Audit complete (PR #28).

Conclusion: the bead's central worry — transitions that need a date 'Skip' — was already resolved by PR #22 (kammerz-b08, Skip dropped) + PR #27 (kammerz-mfj, mapping unified). State machine is clean on that axis:
- Thin statuses (loaded, shooting, at-lab, developing) → STATUS_MILESTONE = null → no prompt → nothing to skip.
- Dated statuses (shot, lab-done, developed, scanned, post-processed, archived) → each has a genuinely meaningful date.
- STATUS_MILESTONE being a total Record enforces classification of any new status at compile time.

PR #28 hardens the two latent silent-advance edges the audit surfaced:
1. at-lab/developing committed status before the dev dialog → dismiss stranded a status with no dev record (and un-editable Timeline rows).
2. lab-done/developed silently advanced when the dev record was missing (reachable via imported orphan).
Fix unifies both dev-entry points onto chooseDevPath's pattern (open dialog, let backend auto_sync_status advance on record creation). Adds devKindForStatus() to status.ts.

Out of scope (left by design): import orphans (historical completed rolls; now recoverable via dialog); archived/post-processed weak-date question; dropped-off vs received asymmetry.

title: Roll detail Shots progress bar: replace raw bg-red-400 with danger token
description:
The Shots-section progress bar in rolls/[id]/+page.svelte (~line 850) uses raw Tailwind 'bg-red-400' for the over-count state, violating the 'never use raw colors' rule in UI_DESIGN.md/CLAUDE.md. The kammerz-r35 film-identity PR (#21) added a token-based over-count indicator (FrameCounter uses --color-danger-fg) right above it, making the inconsistency visible: two over-count visual languages on the same page (token vs raw hex-class).

Pre-existing issue, surfaced during the #21 code review; left out of that PR to keep it scoped to the feature.

Fix: swap 'bg-red-400' -> the danger token (e.g. 'bg-danger') so the over-count bar matches the rest of the theme. Optional: the hero FrameCounter now shows current/total, so the redundant '{frameProgress.current}/{frameProgress.total}' text label in the Shots header could be dropped — but that's a visible change, decide separately.
notes: Location: frontend/src/routes/(app)/rolls/[id]/+page.svelte, Shots section header (~lines 845-855). Related: kammerz-r35 (PR #21).

title: Render the page vignette behind content so it can be richer without hurting text contrast
description:
The photographic vignette (frontend/src/app.css, body::before) is currently a fixed overlay that paints OVER all content at z-index 9998. Because it darkens whatever scrolls under it, the code review (PR #17) had to cap it at 0.12 alpha (and start it at 70%) so it could not pull faint text in a corner below WCAG AA — at 0.3 it dropped text-faint to ~3.2:1. The result is a weaker vignette than originally liked.

A richer vignette is possible WITHOUT the contrast cost by moving the darkening BEHIND content: opaque text and card/sidebar backgrounds then sit on top untouched, while the darkening only shows through the bare page-surface areas. Text on a darker background has equal-or-higher contrast, never lower.

Approaches to evaluate:
- Make the page background itself a radial gradient (e.g. on the root/layout container or `body`) instead of a flat `--color-surface`, with darker edges — rather than a top overlay.
- OR keep a pseudo-element but give it a low/negative z-index so it sits behind app content (verify the (app) layout do not paint an opaque full-bleed background that would fully cover it).

Gotchas:
- The (app) layout / root container may set an opaque `bg-surface` that would hide a behind-content vignette entirely — check frontend/src/routes layout structure first.
- Cards (bg-surface-raised) and the sidebar are opaque and will cover the vignette in their areas; the effect will mainly show in the main-content gaps and page edges. Confirm it is still visible enough to be worth it.
- Whatever the approach, re-run the contrast assertion: text directly on the page surface (section labels, etc.) must still clear WCAG AA against the darkened-corner background.

Outcome: a stronger, moodier vignette that does not dim any foreground text.
acceptance_criteria: Vignette renders behind foreground content (or as the page background); it can be visibly stronger than the current 0.12 overlay; all text — including text-faint on the bare page surface at the darkened corners — still clears WCAG AA 4.5:1; verified in the running app.
close_reason: Vignette moved behind content (root canvas radial gradient); merged in PR #20

title: Explore adding visual interest / liveliness to the UI (build on the accessible theme)
description:
User feedback (2026-06-02) during the accessibility theme work: the UI feels "pretty boring" and could use some interesting/distinctive UI elements. This is intentionally SEPARATE from the readability/colorblind theme work (that one is the UX foundation and the stated priority). Liveliness is open-ended and subjective, so it deserves its own brainstorm rather than riding along with an accessibility fix.

Constraints / guidance:
- Must BUILD ON the new accessible theme (brighter text tiers, red/green-safe status palette), not undo it. Visual interest must not reintroduce low-contrast or hue-only signaling.
- Keep the "Darkroom Ledger" identity (warm near-black surfaces, amber safelight accent, film-grain texture, the serif/mono/sans type system).
- Candidates to explore: richer entrance/transition motion, more characterful empty states, a more distinctive dashboard, texture/depth on cards, a signature visual motif (film sprockets, frame counters, contact-sheet layouts), iconography. Pick a few high-impact moments rather than scattering effects.
- UX correctness and readability win over decoration every time.
acceptance_criteria: Its own brainstorm + spec; a small set of high-impact, on-brand visual enhancements that demonstrably do not regress contrast/colorblind accessibility.

title: Transactional delete-of-missing returns 422 not 404 (shots, lab/self dev)
description: The 3 transactional delete handlers — shots.rs::delete_one, development.rs::delete_lab_dev, development.rs::delete_self_dev — look up the row inside the txn closure with .ok_or_else(|| DbErr::Custom(format!("X {id} not found"))). That DbErr propagates out and is mapped via friendly_err -> AppError::UnprocessableEntity, so DELETE of an already-removed id returns HTTP 422 VALIDATION_ERROR with a misleading 'Could not save X: X {id} not found' message instead of 404 NOT_FOUND. Every non-transactional handler (swept to OptionExt::or_404 in kammerz-19v) correctly returns 404 for the same condition. Pre-existing (predates kammerz-19v); deliberately scoped out of that sweep because the lookup runs inside a DbErr-typed txn closure. Deeper fix: add a From<DbErr> arm classifying DbErr::RecordNotFound as AppError::NotFound plus a DbErr-side or_404 helper, unifying all ~88 sites.
notes: Found by /code-review on kammerz-19v. Real but minor: stale-id double-delete from the frontend gets 422+'could not save' instead of 404. Fix touches error.rs error classification, outside the kammerz-19v diff.
close_reason: Transactional delete-of-missing now returns 404 (PR #31, merged). Added DbOptionExt::or_404_db + friendly_txn_err to classify DbErr::RecordNotFound from txn closures; applied to shots/lab/self delete handlers. 3 tests added.

title: Roll detail: reference lists refetched on every load() call
description: Code-review (efficiency, follow-up to kammerz-0e5): frontend/src/routes/(app)/rolls/[id]/+page.svelte load() re-fetches 5 reference lists (listCameras, listFilmStocks, listLenses, listLabs, listLensMounts) on EVERY call. load() is the sole refresh path — invoked after every shot create/edit/delete and via DevelopmentSection onchange={load} — so each mutation re-pulls all reference data even though it rarely changes. kammerz-0e5 collapsed the roll-scoped round-trips into the composite /detail endpoint but intentionally left these 5 as-is. Caveat: labs and lenses can be created inline on this page, so a naive 'fetch once' cache would go stale after inline creation — the fix must refresh those caches (or just the affected list) after an inline create. Options: (a) split load() into loadRefData() [once on mount] + loadRollData() [on mutation], invalidating ref caches only after inline creates; (b) fold reference data into a composite bootstrap. Pre-existing from the Tauri version; behavior-preserving optimization.
close_reason: Split roll-detail load() into loadRefData() (5 reference lists, once on mount) + loadRollData() (composite /detail, on every mutation). The issue's inline-create caveat was found not to apply — lens/lab dropdowns are read-only Selects, no inline creation on this page — so no cache invalidation needed. Verified: svelte-check 0/0, prod build, and Playwright network trace (ref lists fetched once, only /detail re-fires after a roll-edit mutation; ref data reloads on SPA remount).

title: Change server default port from 3001 to 3002
description: Make the axum server's default listen port 3002 (currently 3001). Update every place the default appears so dev + docs stay consistent: src/main.rs (PORT fallback unwrap_or(3001)); frontend/vite.config.ts (the /api proxy target's 'Number(process.env.PORT) || 3001'); justfile (dev/comments referencing :3001); .env.example (PORT=3001); README quickstart; CLAUDE.md (Commands/Architecture mention :3001). The PORT env var still overrides. After the change, re-run the embedded smoke + Playwright e2e (E2E_BASE/PORT) against the new default.
close_reason: Changed default server port 3001→3002 across main.rs, vite/playwright/smoke configs, justfile, .env.example, README, CLAUDE.md. Verified: cargo build, bun run check, runtime smoke (default binds 3002, PORT override intact).

title: Auth hardening: login rate-limit + evaluate DB pool size
description: Code-review (security/efficiency, non-blocking): (1) POST /api/auth/login has no rate limiting — a shared-password brute-force over LAN/VPN is bounded only by argon2 cost; consider a tower governor/backoff. (2) db.rs uses a max=min=1 SQLite pool (intentional for FK-during-migration determinism + in-memory tests), which serializes all requests; if concurrency ever matters, evaluate a small read pool with pragmas via after_connect. Both are accepted tradeoffs for a single-user LAN app today.
design: Evaluated DB pool: keep max=min=1. A multi-conn read pool needs a per-connection after_connect hook (WAL/foreign_keys are per-connection in SQLite) to preserve the FK-OFF->migrate->ON invariant, and gives no concurrency benefit for a single-user LAN catalog (SQLite serializes writes anyway). No change; rate-limiting is the only code deliverable.
close_reason: Login rate-limiting shipped (tower-governor per-IP throttle + e2e auth-once) and DB-pool evaluated (kept max=min=1, documented). PR #32 merged.

title: Roll detail page: use composite getRollDetail() endpoint
description: Code-review (efficiency): frontend/src/routes/(app)/rolls/[id]/+page.svelte fires 9 parallel + 2 sequential API calls in load(), but the composite GET /api/rolls/{id}/detail (getRollDetail() wrapper) was built to collapse the roll/shots/shot-lens/lab-dev/self-dev/stages round-trips into one and currently has 0 call sites (dead). Rewire the page to use it. Pre-existing from the Tauri version; behavior-affecting so needs testing.
close_reason: Rewired roll detail page load() to use composite getRollDetail() endpoint, collapsing 6 roll-scoped round-trips (roll/shots/shot-lens/lab-dev/self-dev/stages) into one. 5 reference-list lookups remain parallel. Verified: svelte-check 0 errors, prod build ok, browser render of populated roll + 404 not-found path both correct.

title: Reduce route-handler boilerplate (From<DbErr>, now_string, find_or_404)
description: Code-review findings: 53 read handlers use '.map_err(|e| AppError::Internal(e.to_string()))' but error.rs already has 'impl From<DbErr> for AppError' producing exactly that — use bare '?'. ~22 copies of chrono::Utc::now().format("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S").to_string() — add patch::now_string(). 10 update handlers repeat 'fetch-or-404' — add a find_or_404 helper. All behavior-preserving cleanups deferred from the port; do as one mechanical sweep with tests.
close_reason: Mechanical behavior-preserving sweep: 53 map_err(Internal) → bare ? (via existing From<DbErr>), 22 timestamp literals → patch::now_string(), 10 fetch-or-404 → OptionExt::or_404. cargo build + 26 tests + frontend build + svelte-check all green.

title: Add logout UI control to Sidebar
description: auth.svelte.ts store has logout() but no UI wires it. Add a sign-out control to the Sidebar that calls auth.logout() and redirects to /login. Deferred from Phase 4 of the web port.

title: Style the SvelteKit error boundary — replace the bare '500 Internal Error' page
description: The app has no +error.svelte and no custom src/error.html, so any uncaught load/render error falls through to SvelteKit's built-in default error component: an unstyled '500 / Internal Error' on the bare page background, with no app chrome, theme, message, or way back. Observed on /rolls/[id]/print?autoprint=1, but it is app-wide — the print route uses a +page@ layout reset, so it bottoms out at the ROOT error boundary, and the (app) routes have no error boundary either. A first-release app showing a raw framework 500 reads as broken. Add a styled frontend/src/routes/+error.svelte (covers the layout-reset print route and is the catch-all) using the Darkroom Ledger design system: the app background/typography, a friendly message, the status code/message from $page.error, and a 'Back home' link (and/or back to the referring roll). Consider a second +error.svelte inside (app)/ so in-chrome errors render within the sidebar layout rather than resetting to a bare page. Note: page-level data-fetch failures in routes that try/catch (e.g. the print page sets a local error state) already render inline — this boundary is for the uncaught/framework path.
design: Root frontend/src/routes/+error.svelte is the minimum and covers the reset print route. $page.error.message + $page.status give the text. Reuse design-system tokens (bg-surface, text-text-*, accent link) and likely the existing EmptyState/Button components for consistency. Optionally add (app)/+error.svelte so authenticated-section errors keep the sidebar. Keep it static (ssr=false SPA) — no load logic.
acceptance_criteria: Triggering an uncaught route error (e.g. a route that throws, or a 500 from a load) renders a themed page matching the app design, showing a friendly message + status and a link back, instead of the bare 'Internal Error' text. The print route's layout-reset error path is covered by the root +error.svelte.

title: Print summary auto-fires window.print() on every visit, not just first mount
description: The roll print-summary page (frontend/src/routes/(app)/rolls/[id]/print/+page@.svelte, onMount ~line 112-121) calls requestAnimationFrame(() => requestAnimationFrame(() => window.print())) after load. The 'printed' guard only blocks a second fire within the SAME mount — so every navigation to /rolls/[id]/print (including browser back/forward to the URL, or returning to print a second roll) re-opens the modal browser print dialog. There is no way to land on the page to read/copy it without the dialog appearing. Discovered during browser verification of PR #84: the auto-print modal fully wedged a headed Playwright Chromium (every tool call timed out at 30s until the OS process was killed), which also makes the route hostile to automated/e2e coverage. The page already has a visible 'Print' button and a 'Back to roll' link, so an explicit print path exists. Not a correctness bug — printing produces a clean PDF — but the every-visit auto-dialog is aggressive UX.
design: Options: (a) drop the auto-window.print() entirely and rely on the visible Print button (simplest, most predictable); (b) gate auto-print behind a query param (e.g. /rolls/[id]/print?autoprint=1) so the detail-page 'Print summary' button opts in but a direct visit does not; (c) one-shot per session via sessionStorage keyed by roll id. Option (b) preserves the one-click 'click Print summary → dialog opens' flow from the detail page while making bare visits quiet, and keeps the route automatable (omit the param in e2e). Whichever is chosen, ensure e2e/Playwright can load the route without the modal.
acceptance_criteria: Navigating directly to /rolls/[id]/print (or back/forward to it) does NOT auto-open the print dialog; the visible Print button still works; the detail-page 'Print summary' entry point still produces a one-interaction path to the dialog if that flow is retained; the route is loadable under Playwright without a hang.

title: create_shot allows empty/whitespace frame_number — stricter import path now diverges
description: kammerz-42q added pre-validation to the import path: empty/whitespace frame_number is rejected with a targeted 422. The per-shot create path (src/routes/shots.rs create_shot) only trims frame_number and does not reject an empty result, so a shot with an empty frame number can still be created via POST /api/shots. Bring the two paths to parity by rejecting empty/whitespace frame_number in create_shot (and update_shot if applicable) with a 422 validation error. Found during kammerz-42q cross-review.
close_reason: Covered by kammerz-grd server-side frame_number guard (PR #78)

title: Roll-full nudge dismissal resets on every mutation reload
description: loadRollData unconditionally sets rollFullDismissed = false (frontend/src/routes/(app)/rolls/[id]/+page.svelte:277), and loadRollData runs after every mutation on the page (shot add/edit/delete, roll edit, dev changes). So when a roll is full but still 'shooting', dismissing the 'Roll complete' banner (line 911-915) only lasts until the next edit — e.g. dismiss, then fix a shot's notes, and the banner reappears. The adjacent finishDate seeding got a per-roll guard for exactly this same-roll-reload clobbering problem (finishDateSeededFor, lines 282-285), but the dismissal flag did not, which suggests an oversight rather than intent. Suggested fix: guard the reset the same way (only reset rollFullDismissed when navigating to a different roll id), mirroring the finishDateSeededFor pattern.
notes: verifier: confirmed — loadRollData unconditionally resets rollFullDismissed=false at rolls/[id]/+page.svelte:277 and runs on every mutation refresh, while the adjacent finishDate seeding is guarded per-roll (finishDateSeededFor, lines 282-285); the asymmetry reads as an oversight and the dismiss-then-edit-reappears repro is real. P4 calibrated — minor annoyance with a one-line fix.

title: Backend does not enforce lab/self dev mutual exclusivity (UI-only invariant)
description:
CLAUDE.md declares 'Lab and self dev are mutually exclusive' but only the UI enforces it (hides the '+ Lab' / '+ Self' buttons once one exists). The API happily accepts both:

- POST /api/development/lab (src/routes/development.rs:147-187) and POST /api/development/self (development.rs:304-358) never check for a sibling record on the same roll. The UNIQUE indexes from m20260222_000019 (idx_dev_lab_roll_unique / idx_dev_self_roll_unique) are per-table, so one lab dev AND one self dev can coexist on a roll.
- With both present, the status sync becomes ambiguous: sync_lab_dev_status no-ops if the roll sits on the self flow (developing/developed are not on LAB_FLOW, src/services/roll_service.rs:85-106), so the second record is created but the status silently ignores it; the delete handlers already anticipate the dual state (the has_self_dev / has_lab_dev sibling checks at development.rs:269-283 and 458-472).

Why it matters: low day-to-day risk (single user via the UI) but it's the one documented data invariant the backend doesn't own, and the project convention is explicitly that the backend owns status/data-state logic. A stray curl or future bulk-import (kammerz-7zy) could create rolls in the ambiguous dual state.

Suggested fix: in create_lab_dev / create_self_dev, inside the existing transaction, count the sibling table for roll_id and return a 422 ('This roll already has a self development record. Delete it first.') before inserting. Cheap now; breaking-change-free since it's pre-release.
close_reason: verifier: duplicate — same finding (create_lab_dev/create_self_dev at development.rs:147/304 never check the sibling table; UNIQUE indexes are per-table) is tracked in kammerz-ysw (P1, verifier-confirmed, with the stranded-status consequences worked out) and also overlaps kammerz-hbv. Claim is valid but triple-tracked; ysw is the canonical bead.

title: Forward status date prompt has no skip path — unknown date blocks the transition
description: Advancing into a date-bearing status whose date is unset opens DateConfirm (rolls/[id]/+page.svelte:563-570). In transition mode (allowClear=false) the Confirm button is disabled while the field is empty (DateConfirm.svelte:44-46: 'confirming nothing would be a backdoor Skip, which the design deliberately dropped'), and Cancel aborts the entire status change. So a user who scanned or archived a roll but does not remember the date cannot advance at all without entering a made-up date. The sanctioned workaround — enter a date, then clear it from the Timeline afterwards — exists only in a code comment (DateConfirm.svelte:4-6) and is invisible in the UI. The app even has a 'Fuzzy Date' concept for exactly this uncertainty (rolls/new/+page.svelte:257-262), so forcing date precision here is inconsistent. Why it matters: backfilling old rolls (likely heavy at first release when importing a back-catalog) hits this constantly. Suggested fix: add a tertiary 'Advance without date' action to the transition prompt (commits the status with no date write — Timeline already renders undated milestones fine), or at minimum a hint line ('You can clear or change this later in the Timeline').
notes: verifier: adjusted P3->P4 — the no-skip mechanics are real (DateConfirm.svelte:46 disables Confirm on empty, Cancel aborts the transition), but the central 'forces a fake date / inconsistent with Fuzzy Date' claim is wrong: the prompt's gate is dateFieldError (utils/date.ts), which accepts fuzzy YYYY and YYYY-MM, so an uncertain user can confirm '2024' — the Fuzzy Date concept works right in this prompt. The prompt also seeds today, so live use is one click. Remaining issue is discoverability only: DateInput placeholder says YYYY-MM-DD and DateConfirm passes no hint, so fuzzy support and the clear-from-Timeline path are invisible. A hint line suffices; truly-unknown-even-the-year is a rare backfill edge.

title: Remove unused deprecated statusOrder alias and the CLAUDE.md rule preserving it
description: frontend/src/lib/utils/status.ts:140 exports a deprecated alias (export const statusOrder = allStatusOrder). A case-insensitive repo grep shows ZERO consumers — every caller already uses allStatusOrder or getFlowForPath() (frontend/src/routes/(app)/+page.svelte:15, rolls/[id]/+page.svelte:30). Meanwhile CLAUDE.md instructs agents that the alias 'exists for backward compatibility — do not remove it', which preserves dead code forever. Why it matters for first release: the app has never shipped and nothing imports the alias, so backward compatibility protects nobody; the doc rule actively prevents cleanup and is the kind of frozen exception that accumulates. Suggested fix: delete the two lines in status.ts (139-140) and the corresponding CLAUDE.md bullet sentence; bun run check will confirm no consumer breaks.
notes: verifier: confirmed — case-insensitive grep of frontend/src finds zero references to the bare statusOrder alias (every hit is allStatusOrder); status.ts:139-140 exports it and CLAUDE.md's 'don't remove it' bullet preserves dead code in a never-released app. P4 calibrated (two-line cleanup plus one doc sentence).

title: Purge expired rows from the tower_sessions table (deletion task never spawned)
